docs.title: File I/O
docs.text:
• presets The presets object in Pure Data allows users to save and recall multiple configurations of patch parameters, including GUI elements, numbers, symbols, and arrays.
  • midi The midi object in Pure Data is a versatile tool for playing, recording, and manipulating raw MIDI data streams.
  • model The model object in Pure Data renders 3D models saved in the Alias/Wavefront OBJ format.
  • brane.m~ The brane.m~ object is a comprehensive granulator sampler with stretch/compress capabilities and a harmonizer pitch shifter.
  • scala The scala object imports musical scales from .scl files, a format used by the Scala software for microtonal tunings.
  • sfload The sfload object loads sound files into Pure Data arrays, supporting various audio formats like MP3, WAV, and FLAC.
  • sfont.m~ The sfont.m~ object is a convenient wrapper around sfont~ designed for soundfont-based synthesis.
  • pix_writer The pix_writer object saves the current GEM texture (image) to a file on disk.
  • gran.player~ gran.player~ is a granular audio player that allows for independent time stretching and pitch shifting of loaded sound files.
  • capture~ The capture~ object is designed for signal debugging and investigation, capturing audio samples into an internal buffer.
  • sf-play_record The sf-play_record object provides a hard disk recording and playback system for Pure Data, enabling multi-track audio recordings.
  • rec The rec object records incoming messages across multiple tracks and plays them back.
  • sfont~ sfont~ is a sampler synthesizer that plays SoundFont (.sf2/.sf3) files, based on FluidLite.
  • pix_buffer_filmopen The pix_buffer_filmopen object reads a movie file into a named pix_buffer for subsequent image processing within GEM.
  • glsl_geometry The glsl_geometry object loads and compiles a GLSL geometry shader from a specified file.
  • msgfile The msgfile object reads and writes messages to text files, extending the functionality of textfile.
  • pix_multiimage The pix_multiimage object loads multiple image files (TIFF, JPEG, PNG) into memory, which can then be used as textures or for bitblitting within GEM.
  • pix_movie The pix_movie object loads and plays digital video files, making their frames available as textures for real-time graphics rendering within GEM.
  • score The score object is a Pure Data score sequencer that uses a custom text-based syntax to define musical events.
  • presets.m The presets.m object in Pure Data enables saving and recalling parameter presets for "MERDA modules" within a patch.
  • modelfiler The modelfiler object loads 3D model data from Alias/Wavefront OBJ files into Pure Data tables.
  • pix_record The pix_record object in Pure Data is used to record sequences of video frames (pixes) to an output file, pipe, or video device.
  • freadln The freadln object reads text files line by line, outputting each line as a list.
  • buffer~ The buffer~ object stores audio data in memory as a multichannel array, supporting up to 64 channels.
  • play.file~ play.file~ is a Pure Data object that reads and plays audio files from your computer, supporting various formats like AAC, FLAC, MP3, and WAV.
  • sample~ The sample~ object creates and manages an audio buffer, enabling users to load, record, save, and manipulate audio samples.
  • text3d The text3d object in Pure Data renders a single line of text within a 3D graphical environment, leveraging GEM's transformations.
  • funbuff The funbuff object stores and manages ordered pairs of x/y integer values.
  • multimodel The multimodel object loads and renders multiple 3D Alias/Wavefront (.obj) models.
  • seq The seq object in Pure Data is a MIDI sequencer that can play, record, load, and save MIDI streams to and from MIDI or text files.
  • pix_image The pix_image object loads image files (like TIFFs and JPEGs) into Pure Data, primarily for use as textures or for bitblitting operations within the GEM environment.
  • pic The pic object in Pure Data displays image files (.gif, .ppm, .pgm) within a patch.
  • pix_fiducialtrack The pix_fiducialtrack object detects and tracks fiducial markers in black and white grayscale images, utilizing a system similar to reacTable.
  • coll The coll object in Pure Data is a versatile data collection and manipulation tool.
  • pix_imageInPlace The pix_imageInPlace object efficiently loads multiple image files (TIFF, JPEG, PNG) into texture RAM, enabling very fast switching between them.
  • mtr The mtr object in Pure Data functions as a multi-track message recorder and playback system.
  • dir The dir object allows you to access and manage files within directories.
  • pix_film The pix_film object loads and plays digital video files, providing frames as textures for use within Pure Data's GEM environment.
  • capture The capture object stores incoming messages (floats and symbols) in the order they are received, acting as a sequential data buffer.
  • glsl_vertex The glsl_vertex object loads and compiles a GLSL vertex shader into a module.
  • openfile The openfile object in Pure Data is a utility that allows users to open local folders, files, or web links.
  • batch.rec~ batch.rec~ is a convenient Pure Data abstraction for batch recording audio signals to a sound file.
  • sfz~ The sfz~ object is a Pure Data synthesizer that plays SFZ instruments, which are text-based definitions for sample-based and hybrid synthesis.
  • rec.file~ The rec.file~ object records incoming audio signals to a sound file, serving as a convenient wrapper around writesf~.
  • sfinfo The sfinfo object queries information from sound files, such as the number of channels or instrument metadata for AIFF files.
  • pvoc.player~ The pvoc.player~ object is an audio file player that offers independent time stretching and pitch shifting capabilities through a phase vocoder.
  • player~ player~ is a versatile Pure Data object for playing back audio files.
  • pix_buffer pix_buffer is a named storage object for images, functioning similarly to a table but without direct visual access to its contents.
  • fwriteln The fwriteln object writes text files line by line, serving as a simplified version of textfile.
  • score2 The score2 object is a sequencer that plays back musical scores using a rhythmic notation syntax similar to pattern.
  • pix_write The pix_write object captures a snapshot of the current GEM frame buffer and saves it to a file.

docs.title: batch.rec~
docs.text:
batch.rec~ is a convenient Pure Data abstraction for batch recording audio signals to a sound file. It simplifies the process of capturing audio by allowing users to specify a recording duration in milliseconds and a target file name, either as an argument or via a dialog box. Upon completion, it outputs a bang, making it suitable for automated recording workflows.

docs.title: capture~
docs.text:
The capture~ object is designed for signal debugging and investigation, capturing audio samples into an internal buffer. These samples can then be viewed in a text window or saved to a text file for analysis. It allows configuration of the number of samples to store, decimal precision, and capture mode (first N samples or continuous).

docs.title: dir
docs.text:
The dir object allows you to access and manage files within directories. It can open specified directories (absolute or relative paths), list their contents, and filter files by extension. It provides messages to query the current directory, count files, and iterate through them, facilitating file system navigation and content loading.

docs.title: freadln
docs.text:
The freadln object reads text files line by line, outputting each line as a list. It operates sequentially, providing a bang when the end of the file is reached, and does not load the entire file into memory, making it suitable for large files where random access is not required.

docs.title: glsl_vertex
docs.text:
The glsl_vertex object loads and compiles a GLSL vertex shader into a module. It outputs an ID for this module, which can then be linked with the glsl_program object to create custom OpenGL shaders. This object is essential for advanced graphics programming within GEM, requiring OpenGL 2.0 and GLSL support.

It allows loading OBJ files and provides control over rendering properties such as scaling, smoothing, material usage, group visibility, and texture mapping. This object is primarily used for displaying 3D graphics within GEM patches.

It allows for in-place editing of file content, including inserting, appending, deleting, and replacing lines. It also provides navigation, searching, and debugging features, and can handle CSV files.

docs.title: sfload
docs.text:
The sfload object loads sound files into Pure Data arrays, supporting various audio formats like MP3, WAV, and FLAC. It can handle multichannel audio, download files directly from web links, and operates in a threaded mode for efficient loading of large files.

docs.title: Filters
docs.text:
• biquads~ The biquads~ object implements a series of cascaded biquad filters, allowing for complex frequency shaping.
  • mavg The mavg object implements a moving average filter, smoothing incoming float values by averaging a specified number of recent samples.
  • allpass.2nd~ The allpass.2nd~ object is a 2nd order allpass filter that shifts the phase of an audio signal from 0 to 360 degrees across the frequency spectrum.
  • pz2coeff The pz2coeff object converts pole and zero coordinates into biquad filter coefficients.
  • pluck~ The pluck~ object implements the Karplus-Strong algorithm for physical modeling synthesis, simulating plucked string sounds.
  • resonant~ resonant~ is a bandpass resonator filter that allows its resonance to be controlled by Q factor, decay time (t60), or bandwidth.
  • resonbank~ resonbank~ is a bank of resonators designed for sound synthesis, built upon resonator~ objects.
  • bicoeff2 The bicoeff2 object generates coefficients for Pure Data's native biquad~ object, allowing users to easily create various filter types such as lowpass, highpass, bandpass, and shelving filters.
  • pix_convolve The pix_convolve object applies a convolution kernel to a GEM pix (image).
  • bandstop~ bandstop~ is a 2nd order band reject filter, also known as a notch filter.
  • mindwarp~ The mindwarp~ object performs spectral envelope warping, primarily utilizing frequency shaping.
  • lop.bw~ The lop.bw~ object is a lowpass Butterworth filter abstraction.
  • lores~ lores~ is an inexpensive resonant lowpass filter for audio signals.
  • highshelf~ The highshelf~ object is a 2nd order highshelf filter designed to modify the high-frequency content of an audio signal.
  • resonator~ The resonator~ object acts as a resonant filter, controlled by a central frequency and a t60 resonance time.
  • phaseshift~ The phaseshift~ object is a 2nd order allpass filter that alters the phase of an audio signal without changing its gain.
  • lag~ The lag~ object is a one-pole audio filter that applies an exponential glide or portamento to changes in its signal input.
  • reson~ reson~ is a bandpass resonant filter designed for audio signals.
  • hip.bw~ hip.bw~ is a highpass Butterworth filter designed for audio signals.
  • xsyn~ The xsyn~ object performs cross synthesis by filtering its first audio input with the spectral characteristics of its second audio input.
  • circuit~ The circuit~ object simulates analog electronic circuits from a text description.
  • slide~ The slide~ object logarithmically smooths an incoming audio signal, providing non-linear transitions between signal value changes.
  • resonator2~ The resonator2~ object is a resonant complex one-pole filter, designed to be excited by impulses.
  • teeth~ The teeth~ object implements a comb filter, processing an audio signal with independent feedforward and feedback delays.
  • burrow~ The burrow~ object performs cross-filtering on an audio input, using a second audio input as a filter source.
  • glide2~ glide2~ is a signal processing object that smooths incoming signals with distinct ramp-up and ramp-down times, effectively creating a portamento or lag effect.
  • allpass.filt~ The allpass.filt~ object implements an allpass filter, allowing control over its order, central frequency, and resonance (Q).
  • highpass~ The highpass~ object is a 2nd order highpass resonant filter designed for audio signals.
  • filterdelay~ The filterdelay~ object is a high-level audio delay unit that incorporates a resonant lowpass filter, a soft clipper, and a DC filter within its feedback path.
  • plaits.m~ The plaits.m~ object is a versatile polyphonic sound synthesis module, wrapping the [plaits~] external.
  • drown~ The drown~ object performs spectral noise floor manipulation on audio signals using FFT.
  • brickwall~ The brickwall~ object is a 10th order Butterworth lowpass filter with a very steep cutoff slope (60 dB/octave).
  • crossover~ The crossover~ object implements a 3rd order Butterworth crossover filter.
  • bicoeff The bicoeff object is a graphical user interface (GUI) that generates coefficients for vanilla Pure Data's biquad~ filter.
  • multyq~ multyq~ is a four-band spectral equalizer designed for audio processing.
  • bandpass~ The bandpass~ object is a 2nd order resonant bandpass filter designed for audio signals, maintaining a constant 0dB gain.
  • lowshelf~ The lowshelf~ object implements a 2nd order lowshelf filter, designed to adjust the gain of frequencies below a specified shelving frequency.
  • svfilter~ The svfilter~ object implements Chamberlin's state-variable filter algorithm.
  • onepole~ The onepole~ object implements a simple, efficient one-pole IIR low-pass filter, providing 6dB per octave attenuation.
  • resonbank2~ resonbank2~ is a bank of resonant filters designed for sound synthesis.
  • bpbank~ bpbank~ creates a bank of bandpass filters, allowing independent control over the frequency, amplitude, and Q (resonance) of each filter via list inputs.
  • dentist~ The dentist~ object functions as a "spiky" filter, selectively allowing certain partials to pass while attenuating others.
  • comb~ The comb~ object implements a comb filter, an audio effect that combines a signal with a delayed version of itself to create a series of regularly spaced notches or peaks in the frequency response.
  • comb.rev~ The comb.rev~ object implements a comb filter, functioning as a versatile audio effect for creating delays, reverberation, and resonant textures.
  • decay~ decay~ is a one-pole filter that generates an exponential decay from input impulses.
  • svf~ The svf~ object implements a Chamberlin state-variable filter, simultaneously outputting lowpass, highpass, bandpass, and notch filtered signals.
  • vcf.m~ vcf.m~ is a single-channel lowpass filter module.
  • eq~ The eq~ object is a 2nd order parametric equalizer filter, capable of functioning as both a peak and a notch filter.
  • buffir~ The buffir~ object is a table/buffer-based Finite Impulse Response (FIR) filter.
  • pix_tIIR The pix_tIIR object implements a time-based Infinite Impulse Response (IIR) filter for image processing within GEM.
  • allpass~ The allpass~ object implements an all-pass filter, which passes all frequencies without altering their gain but changes their phase.
  • mov.avg~ The mov.avg~ object calculates a moving (or running) average of an incoming audio signal.
  • lowpass~ The lowpass~ object is a 2nd-order resonant lowpass filter designed for audio signals.
  • zbiplot The zbiplot object visualizes biquad filter coefficients by plotting their poles and zeros on the Z-plane.
  • lop2~ lop2~ is a 1st order lowpass filter (1-pole, 1-zero) designed for audio signals.
  • allpass.rev~ The allpass.rev~ object functions as an allpass filter, a delay, and a reverberator.
  • comb.filt~ The comb.filt~ object implements a feedback comb filter, designed primarily for use as a resonator.
  • median~ The median~ object calculates the median value of an incoming audio signal over a specified number of samples.

docs.title: allpass.2nd~
docs.text:
The allpass.2nd~ object is a 2nd order allpass filter that shifts the phase of an audio signal from 0 to 360 degrees across the frequency spectrum. Its behavior is controlled by a center frequency and a resonance (Q or bandwidth) parameter. When mixed with the original signal, it can create comb filtering effects by phase opposition.

docs.title: allpass.filt~
docs.text:
The allpass.filt~ object implements an allpass filter, allowing control over its order, central frequency, and resonance (Q). It functions as an abstraction, cascading multiple allpass.2nd~ objects to achieve the desired filter order, primarily for phase manipulation without altering the frequency response.

docs.title: bandpass~
docs.text:
The bandpass~ object is a 2nd order resonant bandpass filter designed for audio signals, maintaining a constant 0dB gain. It allows frequencies around a specified center frequency to pass, with adjustable resonance via Q factor or bandwidth. It also includes bypass and clear messages for control.

docs.title: bandstop~
docs.text:
bandstop~ is a 2nd order band reject filter, also known as a notch filter. It allows users to filter out a specific range of frequencies from an audio signal, with adjustable center frequency and resonance (Q or bandwidth). It also inherently filters out 0Hz and Nyquist frequencies.

docs.title: bicoeff
docs.text:
The bicoeff object is a graphical user interface (GUI) that generates coefficients for vanilla Pure Data's biquad~ filter. Users can interactively click and drag on the GUI to set filter frequency, bandwidth, and gain for various filter types like lowpass, highpass, bandpass, and shelving filters. It outputs a list of 5 coefficients suitable for the biquad~ object.

docs.title: bicoeff2
docs.text:
The bicoeff2 object generates coefficients for Pure Data's native biquad~ object, allowing users to easily create various filter types such as lowpass, highpass, bandpass, and shelving filters. It takes parameters like frequency, Q/slope, and gain, simplifying the process of designing biquad filters without manual coefficient calculation. This object acts as a utility to configure biquad~ for specific filtering needs.

docs.text:

docs.title: bpbank~
docs.text:
bpbank~ creates a bank of bandpass filters, allowing independent control over the frequency, amplitude, and Q (resonance) of each filter via list inputs. It processes an input signal through these filters and can output each filtered band to a separate channel, with optional ramp times for parameter changes.

docs.title: brickwall~
docs.text:
The brickwall~ object is a 10th order Butterworth lowpass filter with a very steep cutoff slope (60 dB/octave). It is primarily used as an anti-aliasing filter, especially in upsampling and filtering techniques. Its cutoff frequency can be set via an argument or its second inlet, defaulting to 0.75 times the Nyquist frequency.

docs.title: buffir~
docs.text:
The buffir~ object is a table/buffer-based Finite Impulse Response (FIR) filter. It convolves an input signal with samples from a specified buffer, effectively applying the buffer's content as an impulse response. This allows for flexible filtering operations, though it only reads the first channel of multichannel buffers.

docs.title: burrow~
docs.text:
The burrow~ object performs cross-filtering on an audio input, using a second audio input as a filter source. It operates based on FFT information, allowing for dynamic filtering controlled by parameters like frequency, threshold (in dB), and a multiplier factor. It supports both normal and inverted filtering modes and can be configured with window size and overlap for its FFT analysis.

docs.title: onepole~
docs.text:
The onepole~ object implements a simple, efficient one-pole IIR low-pass filter, providing 6dB per octave attenuation. It is used for gently smoothing audio signals or control data by rolling off high frequencies. The cutoff frequency can be controlled via a float or signal, with options for hz, linear, or radians input modes.

docs.title: phaseshift~
docs.text:
The phaseshift~ object is a 2nd order allpass filter that alters the phase of an audio signal without changing its gain. It shifts the phase from 0 degrees at 0 Hz to 360 degrees at the Nyquist frequency. The filter's frequency parameter sets the point where the phase shifts to 180 degrees, and the Q parameter controls the steepness of this phase shift.

docs.title: pix_convolve
docs.text:
The pix_convolve object applies a convolution kernel to a GEM pix (image). It takes matrix dimensions as arguments and a convolution kernel as a list, along with an optional scale factor. This allows for various image processing effects like blurring, sharpening, or edge detection.

docs.title: pix_tIIR
docs.text:
The pix_tIIR object implements a time-based Infinite Impulse Response (IIR) filter for image processing within GEM. It applies feedback and feedforward coefficients to create temporal effects on visual data, similar to motion blur. Users can dynamically configure the filter's characteristics by providing coefficients through its inlets.

docs.title: resonant~
docs.text:
resonant~ is a bandpass resonator filter that allows its resonance to be controlled by Q factor, decay time (t60), or bandwidth. Unlike bandpass~, its gain increases with higher Q values, making it suitable for creating resonant effects. It can also be bypassed.

docs.title: resonator2~
docs.text:
The resonator2~ object is a resonant complex one-pole filter, designed to be excited by impulses. It produces two oscillating outputs, one with a cosine and one with a sine initial phase, making it suitable for creating resonant effects and simplified physical models like a 'Spring Model'.

docs.title: resonator~
docs.text:
The resonator~ object acts as a resonant filter, controlled by a central frequency and a t60 resonance time. It offers bandpass (default), lowpass, and highpass modes. Designed to be excited by an impulse signal, it can generate oscillator-like sounds or simulate physical modeling.

docs.title: resonbank2~
docs.text:
resonbank2~ is a bank of resonant filters designed for sound synthesis. It takes an impulse-like excitation signal and applies it through multiple resonators, allowing control over their frequencies (via partials), decay times, and amplitudes. It supports multichannel output, where each resonator can be routed to a separate channel.

docs.title: reson~
docs.text:
reson~ is a bandpass resonant filter designed for audio signals. It allows dynamic control over gain, center frequency, and resonance (Q) through both float and signal inputs. As a recursive filter, it provides a clear message to reset its internal state, which can be useful if the filter becomes unstable.

docs.title: slide~
docs.text:
The slide~ object logarithmically smooths an incoming audio signal, providing non-linear transitions between signal value changes. It is useful for creating smooth audio fades, envelope following, and acting as a low-pass filter. The smoothing times for both rising and falling signals can be controlled via inlets or arguments.

docs.text:
The svfilter~ object implements Chamberlin's state-variable filter algorithm. It simultaneously outputs lowpass, highpass, bandpass, and bandstop filtered signals. Users can control the cutoff/center frequency and Q/resonance via inlets or arguments.

docs.text:

It takes an audio signal as input and allows control over cutoff/center frequency and Q/resonance, with frequency modes including Hertz, linear, and radians.

docs.title: vcf.m~
docs.text:
vcf.m~ is a single-channel lowpass filter module. It processes an audio input, allowing control over its cutoff frequency (in Hz) and resonance (Q factor). The filter's cutoff and resonance parameters can be modulated via dedicated control voltage (CV) inlets, which are influenced by attenuverter controls.

docs.title: xsyn~
docs.text:
The xsyn~ object performs cross synthesis by filtering its first audio input with the spectral characteristics of its second audio input. This allows for the imposition of one sound's timbral qualities onto another.

docs.title: zbiplot
docs.text:
The zbiplot object visualizes biquad filter coefficients by plotting their poles and zeros on the Z-plane. It helps analyze the frequency response and stability of biquad filters. This object is typically used in conjunction with other objects that generate biquad coefficients, such as bicoeff2.

docs.title: General Utilities
docs.text:
• segregate The segregate object sorts incoming messages by their data type, sending them to dedicated outlets.
  • insert The insert object allows you to insert a message or a list of elements into an existing list at a specified index.
  • smooth~ The smooth~ object provides a signal smoothing function, transitioning input signals with linear or exponential interpolation over a specified time in milliseconds.
  • gate The gate object functions as a message router, directing incoming messages from its second inlet to one of its multiple outlets.
  • initmess The initmess object sends a specified message when a Pure Data patch is loaded.
  • cents2scale The cents2scale object converts a list of intervals, expressed in cents, into a scale defined by semitone steps.
  • capture~ The capture~ object is designed for signal debugging and investigation, capturing audio samples into an internal buffer.
  • loop The loop object in Pure Data provides a flexible iteration mechanism, functioning as a counter that can increment or decrement through a specified range.
  • float2sig~ The float2sig~ (or f2s~) object converts control-rate floats or lists of floats into audio-rate signals.
  • nop The nop object in Pure Data acts as a "no operation" pass-through.
  • forward The forward object sends messages to various destinations, allowing the target to change dynamically with each message, similar to using semicolons in message boxes.
  • slice The slice object splits an incoming list into two parts based on a specified split point n.
  • status The status object detects transitions in numerical input.
  • urn The urn object is a unique random number generator.
  • panic The panic object acts as a "panic button" for MIDI notes.
  • changed The changed object outputs its input only when the incoming message differs from the previously received message, effectively filtering out redundant data.
  • tabwriter~ The tabwriter~ object records incoming audio signals into Pure Data arrays.
  • relay The relay object routes incoming messages based on their first element.
  • zexy_index The index object creates and manages a symbol-to-integer map, allowing you to associate unique symbols with numerical indices.
  • rawprint The rawprint object, part of the zexy external library, provides a raw message printing functionality.
  • nyquist~ The nyquist~ object reports the Nyquist frequency (half the audio sample rate) or its corresponding period.
  • date The date object retrieves the current system date and time.
  • peak The peak object compares incoming numbers to a stored "peak" (maximum) value.
  • default The default object sets and manages a default message.
  • swap~ The swap~ object processes incoming audio signals by first converting them to a 16-bit format.
  • glide2 The glide2 object smoothly transitions (glides) an incoming float signal to new values over time, acting as a portamento or slew limiter.
  • polymetro The polymetro object is a polyrhythmic metronome that generates rhythmic pulses based on a base tempo and a polyrhythmic multiplier.
  • tosymbol The tosymbol object converts any incoming message (e.g., floats, lists) into a single symbol message.
  • lastvalue~ The lastvalue~ Pure Data object reports the last received signal value whenever the input signal changes.
  • thresh The thresh object collects incoming numbers and lists, combining them into a single output list if they arrive within a specified time interval (default 10ms).
  • smooth2~ The smooth2~ object provides signal smoothing with independent ramp-up and ramp-down times, useful for creating portamento or glide effects between signal values.
  • tabdump The tabdump object outputs the entire contents of a Pure Data table as a list.
  • presets.m The presets.m object in Pure Data enables saving and recalling parameter presets for "MERDA modules" within a patch.
  • speedlim The speedlim object controls the rate at which messages are passed through.
  • minmax The minmax object takes a list of floating-point numbers as input.
  • zl The zl object in Pure Data is a versatile list processor that manipulates messages containing one or more elements.
  • tap The tap object detects tempo by analyzing the time interval between two consecutive bangs or the duration a gate is held open.
  • canvas.bounds The canvas.bounds object outputs the pixel coordinates (top-left x, top-left y, bottom-right x, bottom-right y) of its Pure Data canvas.
  • bpm The bpm object converts between beats per minute (BPM) and other time units.
  • tabset The tabset object from the zexy library allows users to set the contents of a Pure Data table.
  • route2 The route2 object functions similarly to route, but it preserves the list selector in its output messages and supports both float and symbol arguments for routing.
  • count The count object in Pure Data generates a sequence of numbers, incrementing or decrementing between a defined minimum and maximum value.
  • time The time object outputs the current system time in milliseconds, seconds, minutes, and hours when triggered by a bang.
  • iterate The iterate object splits an incoming message (typically a list of floats or symbols) into its individual elements and outputs them sequentially.
  • sh~ The sh~ object samples and holds an audio signal from its left inlet.
  • datetime The datetime object provides the current local date and time as lists.
  • reverse The reverse object reorders the elements of incoming messages or lists.
  • wrap The wrap object functions as a float-capable modulo, constraining an input float value within specified limits.
  • format The format object creates a single symbol (string) by combining a format string with multiple input values.
  • buddy The buddy object synchronizes incoming messages from its inlets.
  • any2symbol The any2symbol object converts any incoming message, regardless of its type (e.g., numbers, lists, or other symbols), into a single symbol message.
  • smooth The smooth object in Pure Data is designed to smoothly interpolate control signals over a specified time in milliseconds, preventing abrupt changes.
  • meter The meter object functions as a CPU load meter, providing real-time monitoring of the system's processing usage.
  • gaterelease The gaterelease object controls the release of a gate value.
  • unite The unite object concatenates incoming messages and converts them into a single symbol message.
  • setdsp~ The setdsp~ object provides a convenient way to control Pure Data's audio engine (DSP) state.
  • strcmp The strcmp object compares two lists as if they were strings, similar to the strcmp function in C programming.
  • group The group object collects incoming messages and outputs them as a single list once a specified group size is reached.
  • symbol2any The symbol2any object converts an incoming symbol message into a generic 'any' message.
  • routeall The routeall object routes incoming messages to different outlets based on matching arguments, similar to route but with enhanced capabilities.
  • mux The mux object functions as a multiplexer, directing the signal from one of its multiple input inlets to its single outlet.
  • sr~ sr~ is a Pure Data object that reports and can set the global audio sample rate.
  • next The next object in Pure Data detects whether an incoming message is part of the same "logical event" as the previous one.
  • gatehold The gatehold object extends the duration of a gate signal by a specified amount of time (in milliseconds) after the gate has closed.
  • spell The spell object converts incoming messages, character by character, into their corresponding UTF-8 (Unicode) integer values.
  • length The length object, part of the zexy external library, determines the number of elements in an incoming list.
  • break The break object in Pure Data is used to split incoming messages, typically lists or symbols, into multiple parts based on a specified separator symbol.
  • replace The replace object modifies an input message by substituting a specific element at a given index.
  • atoi The atoi object converts an incoming symbol (string) into a numerical representation.
  • counter The counter object in Pure Data increments or decrements an integer value within a specified range.
  • cycle The cycle object distributes incoming messages to its outlets in a round-robin fashion.
  • rad2hz The rad2hz object converts a frequency value from "Radians per Sample" to Hertz.
  • split The split object separates incoming float numbers into two outlets based on whether they fall within a specified minimum and maximum range.
  • selector The selector object routes incoming data from one of its multiple inlets to its single output.
  • amean The amean object generates a list of numbers representing arithmetic means between a specified start and end point.
  • fontsize The fontsize object reports the current font size of a Pure Data patch.
  • count~ The count~ object is a sample-accurate counter that outputs a signal incrementing by one for each elapsed sample.
  • loadbanger The loadbanger (or lb) object in Pure Data sends "bang" messages when a patch is loaded, when it receives any message, or when clicked.
  • operating_system The operating_system object outputs a symbol representing the operating system Pure Data is currently running on.
  • gatehold~ The gatehold~ object holds an incoming gate signal for a specified duration in milliseconds after the gate closes.
  • gaterelease~ The gaterelease~ object releases an audio-rate gate signal after a specified time in milliseconds, allowing for timed gate control even when other gates are held.
  • accum The accum object accumulates a numerical value by either adding an increment or multiplying by a given factor.
  • sig2float~ The sig2float~ object converts audio signals into floating-point numbers.
  • listfunnel The listfunnel object takes an incoming list and outputs its elements sequentially, each preceded by its corresponding index.
  • capture The capture object stores incoming messages (floats and symbols) in the order they are received, acting as a sequential data buffer.
  • canvas.edit The canvas.edit object reports the edit status of a Pure Data canvas, outputting 1 when in edit mode and 0 in run mode.
  • message The message object in Pure Data serves as a versatile container for storing and outputting any type of message, functioning similarly to a message box but as an object.
  • bangdiv The bangdiv object acts as a bang divider, outputting a bang only after a specified number of input bangs have been received, while always passing the very first bang.
  • morph The morph object is an abstraction that extends the functionality of Pure Data's line object to lists of numbers.
  • openfile The openfile object in Pure Data is a utility that allows users to open local folders, files, or web links.
  • substitute The substitute object replaces elements within an input message.
  • dec2hex The dec2hex object converts decimal numbers into their hexadecimal string representation.
  • meter~ The meter~ object functions as a mono VU-meter, providing real-time visual feedback on audio signal levels.
  • atof The atof object converts an ASCII symbol (text) into a floating-point number.
  • rand.dist The rand.dist object generates random numbers based on a user-defined probability density function (PDF) provided by a Pure Data array.
  • scale~ scale~ maps an input signal range to an output signal range, supporting both linear and exponential scaling.
  • separate The separate object in Pure Data splits an input symbol (string) into a list of individual elements.
  • tempo The tempo object functions as a versatile metronome, sending bangs at a specified rate in BPM, milliseconds, or Hertz.
  • cents2ratio~ The cents2ratio~ object converts an audio signal representing an interval in cents into a decimal ratio signal.
  • pick The pick object selects an element from an input message based on a provided element number.
  • loadmess The loadmess object sends a predefined message automatically when its containing patch is loaded, or when it receives a bang message.
  • hex2dec The hex2dec object converts hexadecimal values to their decimal equivalents.
  • iter The iter object splits an incoming message (which can contain floats or symbols) into its individual elements.
  • bangbang The bangbang object outputs a specified number of bang messages from its outlets in right-to-left order when it receives any input.
  • slew The slew object limits the rate of change of an incoming signal, generating a smooth transition (portamento) towards the target value.
  • glide The glide object creates a smooth transition (portamento) for incoming numerical values over a specified time in milliseconds.
  • limit The limit object controls the rate of messages, ensuring a minimum time interval between outputs.
  • nop~ The nop~ object is a signal-level no-operation (NOP) utility from the zexy library.
  • clip The cyclone/clip object constrains incoming float or list values to a specified numerical range.
  • swap2 The else/swap2 object swaps the order of two incoming messages.
  • chrono The chrono object functions as a versatile stopwatch or timer.
  • remove The remove object filters elements from an input list.
  • rescale~ The rescale~ object transforms incoming audio signals from a specified input range to a desired output range.
  • onebang The onebang object acts as a conditional gate for bang messages.
  • uzi The uzi object generates a sequence of bangs and corresponding counter values, useful for iterating through a specified number of steps.
  • bitsafe~ The bitsafe~ object processes incoming audio signals, replacing any "Not a Number" (NaN) or infinity values with zero.
  • else The else object provides information about the ELSE library, including its version, release date, and installation directory.
  • l2s The list2symbol (or l2s) object converts an incoming list into a single symbol.
  • gemargs The gemargs object retrieves arguments and initialization messages passed to its parent patch, enabling consistent object initialization patterns within GEM abstractions.
  • clock The clock object generates bangs at a regular tempo, functioning as either a "main" or "synced" clock.
  • rescale The rescale object maps input values from a specified input range to a new output range.
  • bitnormal~ The bitnormal~ object filters incoming audio signals, replacing any NaN (Not a Number), positive/negative infinity, or denormal values with zero.
  • router The router object routes incoming messages from its left inlet to a specific outlet.
  • order The order object splits an incoming list into successive sub-lists of a specified size, assigning an ordered index to each.
  • notedur2ratio The notedur2ratio object converts musical note duration symbols (e.g., 1n for whole note, 4n for quarter note) into their corresponding numerical ratios, which can be output as fractions or floats.
  • note2midi The note2midi object converts musical note names (e.g., 'C4', 'E#+5', 'Db-4') into their corresponding MIDI pitch values.
  • rad2deg The rad2deg object converts radian values to degrees.
  • list2int The list2int object converts all floating-point numbers within an incoming list to integers.
  • avg The avg object calculates the mean average of all numbers it has received.
  • trig2bang The trig2bang object detects zero-to-non-zero transitions in an incoming signal (typically a float).
  • sprintf The sprintf object in Pure Data formats messages similar to C's printf function.

docs.title: bitsafe~
docs.text:
The bitsafe~ object processes incoming audio signals, replacing any "Not a Number" (NaN) or infinity values with zero. It also filters out denormal numbers, converting them to zero. This is particularly useful before performing bitwise operations or in any context where such problematic signal values might occur.

docs.title: date
docs.text:

docs.title: gatehold
docs.text:
The gatehold object extends the duration of a gate signal by a specified amount of time (in milliseconds) after the gate has closed. If a new gate signal is received before the hold time expires, the object re-triggers, effectively restarting the hold period. This allows for precise control over the timing and sustain of gate-dependent processes, such as envelopes.

docs.text:

docs.title: glide2
docs.text:

It offers independent control over the attack (glide up) and decay (glide down) times in milliseconds, and an exponential factor for shaping the curve. A reset message can instantly set the output to the current input value.

docs.title: limit
docs.text:
The limit object controls the rate of messages, ensuring a minimum time interval between outputs. It can queue the last received message to be sent after the delay, or, depending on its 'ignore mode' argument, it can discard messages that arrive too quickly, sending them to a separate outlet, or restart its internal timer. This is useful for rate-limiting events or data streams.

docs.text:

docs.text:

It can apply either linear or exponential curves to these transitions, effectively creating a portamento or glide effect for control values. This is useful for making control changes sound less jarring, such as when changing the frequency of an osc~ object.

docs.title: Granular
docs.text:
• grain.sampler~ The grain.sampler~ object is a sample-based granulator that generates clouds of audio grains from a loaded sample.
  • brane.m~ The brane.m~ object is a comprehensive granulator sampler with stretch/compress capabilities and a harmonizer pitch shifter.
  • grain.live~ grain.live~ is a live input granulator that generates clouds of audio grains from an incoming signal.
  • grain.synth~ grain.synth~ is a waveform-based granular synthesizer that generates clouds of pitched grains.
  • gran.player~ gran.player~ is a granular audio player that allows for independent time stretching and pitch shifting of loaded sound files.
  • plaits~ The plaits~ object is a versatile audio synthesizer based on the Mutable Instruments Plaits module, offering 24 distinct synthesis engines.
  • pitch.shift~ pitch.shift~ is a pitch shifter object that transposes audio signals up or down.
  • plaits.m~ The plaits.m~ object is a versatile polyphonic sound synthesis module, wrapping the [plaits~] external.
  • pvgrain~ pvgrain~ is a spectral granulator that tracks an input sound and synthesizes grains based on its spectral characteristics.
  • stretch.shift~ The stretch.shift~ object performs real-time independent time stretching and pitch shifting on live audio input.
  • pvoc.live~ The pvoc.live~ object performs independent time stretching and pitch shifting on live audio input using granulation.

docs.title: brane.m~
docs.text:
The brane.m~ object is a comprehensive granulator sampler with stretch/compress capabilities and a harmonizer pitch shifter. It records or plays mono audio files, offering independent pitch shifting for 'root' and 'harmony' voices, and includes an autotuner that can quantize pitches to any scale, with a built-in equal division scale generator. Additional features include automatic looping, bouncing, freezing, audio slice selection, granular synthesis, random playback settings, and a dedicated preset management system.

docs.title: grain.sampler~
docs.text:
The grain.sampler~ object is a sample-based granulator that generates clouds of audio grains from a loaded sample. It offers extensive control over grain parameters such as duration, transposition, panning, amplitude, size, position, and reversal probability. The object supports both asynchronous and synchronous grain generation modes, with an option for autotuning grains to a specified scale.

docs.title: grain.synth~
docs.text:
grain.synth~ is a waveform-based granular synthesizer that generates clouds of pitched grains. It allows control over parameters such as grain duration, size, pitch, amplitude, and panning, and supports both synchronous and asynchronous modes. Users can also define custom grain envelopes and apply tuning scales.

docs.title: gran.player~
docs.text:
gran.player~ is a granular audio player that allows for independent time stretching and pitch shifting of loaded sound files. It supports various file formats and offers control over playback, looping, grain size, and sample range.

docs.title: pitch.shift~
docs.text:
pitch.shift~ is a pitch shifter object that transposes audio signals up or down. It operates using a granulation technique, allowing users to control the transposition factor in cents and the grain size in milliseconds.

docs.title: plaits~
docs.text:
The plaits~ object is a versatile audio synthesizer based on the Mutable Instruments Plaits module, offering 24 distinct synthesis engines. It features core controls for 'Harmonics', 'Timbre', and 'Morph', whose functions adapt to the selected engine, and includes a built-in Low-Pass Gate (LPG) with trigger and level inputs for dynamic sound shaping. It supports various pitch input modes (Hz, MIDI, CV) and extensive modulation capabilities for its synthesis parameters.

docs.title: pvgrain~
docs.text:
pvgrain~ is a spectral granulator that tracks an input sound and synthesizes grains based on its spectral characteristics. It allows control over parameters such as grain duration, frequency range, and the probability of grain generation per FFT frame. The object is designed for audio processing and assumes a 44100 Hz sample rate.

docs.title: pvoc.live~
docs.text:
The pvoc.live~ object performs independent time stretching and pitch shifting on live audio input using granulation. It takes an audio signal as input and allows control over playback speed and pitch transposition in cents, with arguments for buffer size, initial speed, and initial transposition.

docs.title: stretch.shift~
docs.text:
The stretch.shift~ object performs real-time independent time stretching and pitch shifting on live audio input. It achieves this by employing granulation, allowing users to control parameters such as buffer size, playback speed, transposition in cents, and grain size for creative audio manipulation.
