docs.title: Math
docs.text:
• quantizer~ The quantizer~ object quantizes an incoming audio signal to discrete step values, which can be defined by an argument or a second inlet.
  • acos The acos object calculates the arc-cosine (inverse cosine) of a given number.
  • mavg The mavg object implements a moving average filter, smoothing incoming float values by averaging a specified number of recent samples.
  • allpass.2nd~ The allpass.2nd~ object is a 2nd order allpass filter that shifts the phase of an audio signal from 0 to 360 degrees across the frequency spectrum.
  • pix_threshold_bernsen pix_threshold_bernsen applies a dynamic tiled threshold filter to greyscale images for binarization.
  • ratio2cents The ratio2cents object converts rational number intervals (expressed as floating-point decimal values) into cents.
  • pdf~ The pdf~ object calculates the probability density function of an incoming signal.
  • trunc~ The trunc~ object truncates an audio signal towards zero, effectively taking only the integer part of each sample's value.
  • pow~ cyclone/pow~ raises a base value to the power of an exponent.
  • car2pol~ The car2pol~ object converts a signal from Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts) to polar coordinates (amplitude and phase).
  • cents2frac The cents2frac object converts a list of pitch values in cents into fractional representations, which are useful for defining musical intervals as ratios.
  • coeff2pz The coeff2pz object converts biquad filter coefficients into pole and zero coordinates, along with the overall gain of the filter's response.
  • pz2coeff The pz2coeff object converts pole and zero coordinates into biquad filter coefficients.
  • prime The prime object detects if an input number is prime.
  • mean The mean object calculates the arithmetic mean (average) of a list of floating-point numbers.
  • cents2scale The cents2scale object converts a list of intervals, expressed in cents, into a scale defined by semitone steps.
  • pmpd2d The pmpd2d object implements a 2D mass-spring-damper physical model, designed for particle-based simulations in Pure Data.
  • pol2cart The pol2cart object converts 3D polar coordinates (radius r, azimuthal angle phi, and polar angle theta) into Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z).
  • rdiv~ The rdiv~ object, also known as !/~, performs division on audio signals.
  • ikeda~ The ikeda~ object is a chaotic signal generator based on the Ikeda map, producing two audio outputs.
  • op The op object in Pure Data performs a wide range of arithmetic, comparative, logical, and bitwise operations.
  • gemlist_info The gemlist_info object takes a gemlist as input and decomposes its current transformation matrix into basic transformations.
  • floor~ The floor~ object performs a floor mathematical operation on incoming audio signals, rounding each sample down to the nearest integer.
  • autotune2 The autotune2 object retunes incoming pitch values (in cents) to the closest step within a specified musical scale, also defined in cents.
  • henon~ The henon~ object is a chaotic signal generator that implements the Henon map difference equation.
  • wave~ The wave~ object reads audio data from a specified array (wavetable) using a phase signal (0-1) as an index.
  • hsv2rgb The hsv2rgb object converts color values from the Hue, Saturation, Value (HSV) color space to the Red, Green, Blue (RGB) color space.
  • asin~ The asin~ object calculates the arc-sine of an input signal.
  • rdiv The rdiv (or !/) object performs division with reversed inlet behavior compared to the standard / object.
  • pix_add The pix_add object combines two input images of the same size by adding their pixel values.
  • dbtoa~ The dbtoa~ object converts a decibel full scale (dBFS) amplitude signal to a linear amplitude signal.
  • add The add object accumulates incoming numerical values, adding them to a running total.
  • frac2cents The frac2cents object converts musical intervals expressed as fractional symbols (e.g., 9/8) into their corresponding values in cents.
  • maximum The maximum object outputs the largest value from its inputs.
  • cosx~ The cyclone/cosx~ object calculates the cosine of an incoming signal, expecting the input values to be in radians.
  • bitxor~ The bitxor~ object performs a bitwise exclusive OR (XOR) operation on two input signals, or a signal and a given bitmask.
  • linedrive The linedrive object scales numbers from an input range to an output range using an exponential curve.
  • round The round object approximates incoming numbers (floats or lists of floats) to the nearest integer multiple of a specified value.
  • car2pol The car2pol object converts Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts) into polar coordinates (amplitude and phase).
  • bicoeff2 The bicoeff2 object generates coefficients for Pure Data's native biquad~ object, allowing users to easily create various filter types such as lowpass, highpass, bandpass, and shelving filters.
  • atodb~ The atodb~ object converts a linear amplitude audio signal to its equivalent decibel full scale (dBFS) value.
  • sph2cart The sph2cart object converts spherical coordinates (radius, azimuth, elevation) to Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z).
  • mag~ The mag~ object calculates the magnitude (amplitude) or power magnitude of a complex signal from its real and imaginary parts.
  • ceil The ceil object from the else library computes the ceiling of its input.
  • coordinates The cart2pol, pol2sph, sph2cart, cart2sph, pol2cart, and sph2pol objects convert between Cartesian, Polar, and Spherical coordinate systems.
  • deg2rad The deg2rad object converts a float value from degrees to radians.
  • lincong~ The lincong~ object is a chaotic signal generator based on a linear congruential difference equation.
  • atodb The atodb object converts linear amplitude values into decibels full scale (dBFS).
  • mov.rms~ The mov.rms~ object calculates the Root Mean Square (RMS) of an incoming audio signal over a specified moving time window.
  • mass3D The mass3D object simulates a mass in a 3D space, calculating its position and velocity based on applied forces, weight, damping, and boundary conditions.
  • sampstoms~ The sampstoms~ object converts a time value from samples to milliseconds, adjusting for the current sample rate.
  • peak The peak object compares incoming numbers to a stored "peak" (maximum) value.
  • tanh~ The tanh~ object calculates the hyperbolic tangent of an input audio signal.
  • asinh~ The asinh~ object calculates the hyperbolic arc-sine of each incoming audio sample.
  • tCylinder3D The tCylinder3D object defines a 3D cylindrical volume and tests if a given 3D point (referred to as a "mass") is located within its boundaries.
  • rgb2hsv The rgb2hsv object converts color values from the RGB (Red, Green, Blue) color space to the HSV (Hue, Saturation, Value/Luminance) color space.
  • pong~ The pong~ object limits an input audio signal within a specified low-high range.
  • polymetro The polymetro object is a polyrhythmic metronome that generates rhythmic pulses based on a base tempo and a polyrhythmic multiplier.
  • atan2~ The atan2~ object calculates the arctangent of two input signals, 'a' and 'b', as atan(a/b).
  • asinh The asinh object calculates the hyperbolic arc-sine of a given number.
  • mass2D The mass2D object simulates a 2D mass, calculating its position and velocity based on applied forces, weight, and damping.
  • fold~ The fold~ object performs wavefolding, a waveshaping technique that mirrors audio signals back into a specified low and high range.
  • shaper~ The shaper~ object performs waveshaping by applying a user-defined transfer function to an input signal.
  • factor The factor object is a mathematical utility that takes a floating-point number as input.
  • add~ The add~ object accumulates input signal values to a starting sum, which defaults to 0.
  • phasewrap~ The phasewrap~ object wraps an incoming signal's value between -π and π.
  • iCircle2D The iCircle2D object simulates circular interactions in 2D space, applying various forces and displacements to an interacting mass.
  • delta~ The delta~ object calculates the difference between the current and previous sample of an incoming audio signal.
  • minmax The minmax object takes a list of floating-point numbers as input.
  • function~ The function~ object generates arbitrary waveforms or functions from a list of points and periods.
  • minimum~ The minimum~ object outputs a signal that represents the minimum value between two input signals, or between an input signal and a numerical argument.
  • bpm The bpm object converts between beats per minute (BPM) and other time units.
  • sin~ The sin~ object computes the sine of its signal input, scaled by two pi, outputting the result as a signal.
  • scale2freq scale2freq converts a musical scale, provided as a list of cents values, into a corresponding list of frequencies in Hertz.
  • cartopol The cartopol~ object converts Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts) into polar coordinates (amplitude and phase).
  • rminus~ The rminus~ (or !-~) object performs subtraction on audio signals.
  • hann~ The hann~ object applies a Hann window to a signal input, with the window size matching the current block size.
  • cosh~ The cosh~ object calculates the hyperbolic cosine of an input signal sample by sample.
  • iAmbient2D The iAmbient2D object simulates ambient interactions in a 2D space, applying constant, random, and damping forces to named "masses." It also handles displacement if a mass moves outside a defined rectangular area.
  • rminus The rminus (or !-) object performs subtraction with its inlets reversed compared to the standard [-] object.
  • ceil~ The ceil~ object is a signal-rate mathematical function that performs the ceiling operation on incoming audio signals.
  • euclid The euclid object implements the Euclidean rhythm algorithm, which distributes a given number of "hits" as evenly as possible over a specified number of "steps".
  • wrap The wrap object functions as a float-capable modulo, constraining an input float value within specified limits.
  • tanh The tanh object calculates the hyperbolic tangent of its input.
  • db2lin~ The db2lin~ object converts decibel Full Scale (dBFS) amplitude values to linear amplitude values.
  • dec2frac The dec2frac object converts decimal numbers into fractional representations.
  • link The link object calculates forces between two masses, simulating a physical connection with configurable rigidity and damping.
  • rand.f The rand.f object generates pseudo-random floating-point numbers within a user-defined range when triggered by a bang.
  • clip~ The clip~ object constrains an input audio signal to a specified minimum and maximum value.
  • interpolate The else/interpolate object performs smooth interpolation between a set of start and target values, which can be single numbers or lists.
  • sinx~ The sinx~ object calculates the sine of an incoming audio signal.
  • asin The asin object calculates the arc-sine (inverse sine) of a given number.
  • acosh The acosh object calculates the hyperbolic arc-cosine of a given float input.
  • floor The floor object, part of the else library, performs a mathematical floor operation.
  • lorenz~ The lorenz~ object is a chaotic signal generator based on the Lorenz strange attractor, a mathematical model of atmospheric convection.
  • spread~ The spread~ object distributes multiple input audio channels across multiple output channels using equal power panning.
  • hz2rad The hz2rad object converts a frequency value in Hertz (Hz) to "Radians per Sample." This conversion is crucial for digital signal processing, as it calculates the phase increment per sample based on the patch's sample rate using the formula rad = (hz * 2*pi / sr)..
  • polyfun The polyfun object evaluates a polynomial function.
  • pol2car~ The pol2car~ object converts audio signals from polar coordinates (amplitude and phase) to Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts).
  • poltocar The poltocar object converts polar coordinates (amplitude and phase) to Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary).
  • list.inc The list.inc object generates a list of numbers based on a starting value, an increment step, and the desired number of elements.
  • bitand~ The cyclone/bitand~ object performs a bitwise AND operation on two incoming signals or a signal and a given bitmask.
  • dbtoa The dbtoa object converts decibel (dBFS) amplitude values to their corresponding linear amplitude.
  • anal The anal object reports the number of times it receives a specific number pair, effectively building a histogram of occurrences.
  • round~ The round~ object quantizes an incoming audio signal by approximating its values to an integer multiple of a specified number.
  • pulse The pulse object is a control-rate oscillator that generates a pulse wave, acting like a toggle switch.
  • maximum~ The maximum~ object outputs the greater of two input signals, or the greater of an input signal and a given argument.
  • sum~ The sum~ object takes a multi-channel audio signal and sums all its channels into a single mono output signal.
  • wrap2 The wrap2 object constrains a numerical input value to a specified range by "wrapping" it around the minimum and maximum boundaries.
  • cusp~ cusp~ is a chaotic signal generator that implements the difference equation y[n] = a - b * sqrt(abs(y[n-1])).
  • wrap2~ The wrap2~ object wraps an input signal between a specified low and high value.
  • mov.avg The mov.avg object calculates a running or moving average of incoming float values.
  • rand.u The rand.u object generates a sequence of unrepeated random integer values within a specified range (0 to size-1).
  • ms2samps The ms2samps object is a simple utility that converts a time value in milliseconds to the equivalent number of audio samples.
  • frameaccum~ frameaccum~ accumulates the values of each sample across incoming signal blocks, primarily designed for computing a running phase of FFT frames.
  • acosh~ The acosh~ object computes the hyperbolic arc-cosine of an incoming audio signal, sample by sample.
  • rint The rint object rounds floating-point numbers to the nearest integer value.
  • cartopol~ The cartopol~ object converts a complex signal from Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts) to polar coordinates (amplitude and phase).
  • abs~ The abs~ object calculates the absolute value of an incoming audio signal.
  • lin2db The lin2db object converts a linear amplitude value to its equivalent decibel full scale (dBFS) representation.
  • trunc The trunc object truncates floating-point numbers and lists of numbers towards zero, effectively extracting their integer part.
  • sgn~ The sgn~ object calculates the signum of an incoming audio signal, outputting 1 for positive values, -1 for negative values, and 0 for zero.
  • rad2hz The rad2hz object converts a frequency value from "Radians per Sample" to Hertz.
  • latoocarfian~ The latoocarfian~ object is a chaotic audio signal generator that implements the Latoocarfian attractor's difference equations.
  • zigbinops The zigbinops help patch describes a collection of signal-rate Pure Data objects from the zexy library.
  • frac.mul The frac.mul object in Pure Data performs multiplication of two fractional values.
  • cyclone The cyclone library for Pure Data provides a comprehensive set of mathematical and comparative operators, many of which are direct aliases for common Max/MSP objects.
  • amean The amean object generates a list of numbers representing arithmetic means between a specified start and end point.
  • tLine2D The tLine2D object defines a 2D line segment using two points and tracks the position of a "mass" relative to it.
  • rgb2yuv The rgb2yuv object converts color values from the RGB (Red, Green, Blue) color space to the YUV (Luma, Chroma) color space.
  • count~ The count~ object is a sample-accurate counter that outputs a signal incrementing by one for each elapsed sample.
  • pong The pong object in Pure Data is a utility for manipulating numerical inputs.
  • pol2sph The pol2sph object converts 2D polar coordinates (radius, angle, and height) into 3D spherical coordinates.
  • ratio2cents~ The ratio2cents~ object converts a signal representing a rational interval (e.g., a frequency ratio) into an interval expressed in cents.
  • poltocar~ The poltocar~ object converts signal values from polar coordinates (amplitude and phase) to Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts).
  • samps2ms The samps2ms object is a simple abstraction that converts a time value given in audio samples into milliseconds.
  • average~ The average~ object calculates a moving average of an audio signal over a specified number of samples.
  • gbman~ gbman~ is a chaotic signal generator based on the Gingerbread Man map.
  • sinh The sinh object calculates the hyperbolic sine of a given numerical input.
  • sph2pol The sph2pol object converts spherical coordinates (radius R, azimuthal angle phi, and polar angle theta) into polar coordinates.
  • accum The accum object accumulates a numerical value by either adding an increment or multiplying by a given factor.
  • absgn~ The absgn~ object calculates the absolute value and the signum of an incoming audio signal.
  • scalarmult The scalarmult object performs scalar multiplication, also known as a "dot product," on incoming lists of numbers.
  • median The median object calculates the median value of a list of numbers.
  • tavg~ The tavg~ object calculates the arithmetic mean of an incoming audio signal.
  • cross~ The cross~ object performs block convolution for cross-synthesis.
  • trough The trough object compares incoming float values to a stored 'trough' (minimum) value.
  • tSphere3D The tSphere3D object performs a spherical test to determine if a "mass" (likely a point or object) is located within a defined 3D spherical region.
  • bangdiv The bangdiv object acts as a bang divider, outputting a bang only after a specified number of input bangs have been received, while always passing the very first bang.
  • gain~ The gain~ object provides mono and multichannel gain control for audio signals.
  • freq2midi The freq2midi object converts frequency values (Hz) to MIDI note numbers, similar to ftom.
  • morph The morph object is an abstraction that extends the functionality of Pure Data's line object to lists of numbers.
  • framedelta~ The framedelta~ object calculates the running phase deviation of an FFT analysis.
  • power~ power~ is a signal processing object in Pure Data that acts as a power function waveshaper.
  • quad~ The quad~ object generates a chaotic audio signal based on a quadratic difference equation: y[n] = a * y[n-1]^2 + b * y[n-1] + c.
  • mstosamps~ The mstosamps~ object converts a time value in milliseconds to the corresponding number of audio samples, taking the current sample rate into account.
  • pol2car The pol2car object converts polar coordinates (amplitude and phase) into Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts).
  • tanx~ The tanx~ object calculates the tangent of an incoming audio signal, expecting the input values to be in radians.
  • cents2ratio The cents2ratio object converts musical intervals expressed in cents into frequency ratios (floating-point decimals).
  • sinh~ The sinh~ object calculates the hyperbolic sine of an incoming signal.
  • markov The markov object in Pure Data generates sequences based on Markov chains.
  • histogram The histogram object records the frequency of incoming positive integer numbers (floats are truncated) into an internal table.
  • fold The fold object mirrors input values that exceed a defined range back into that range, effectively "folding" them.
  • tabgen tabgen is a Pure Data abstraction that generates various mathematical functions (e.g., sine, Gaussian, sinc, trapezoid, variable sawtooth, noise, and FFT window functions) and populates a specified table (array) with the generated data.
  • scale~ scale~ maps an input signal range to an output signal range, supporting both linear and exponential scaling.
  • mag The mag object calculates the magnitude (amplitude) from Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts), which is fundamental for analyzing frequency spectrum data.
  • mix2~ The mix2~ object is a 2-channel audio mixer that provides independent gain and panning control for each input signal.
  • quantizer The quantizer object approximates input values to discrete step values defined by an argument.
  • cents2ratio~ The cents2ratio~ object converts an audio signal representing an interval in cents into a decimal ratio signal.
  • pi The pi object calculates and outputs the mathematical constant Pi.
  • acos~ The acos~ object calculates the arc-cosine of an incoming signal.
  • hex2dec The hex2dec object converts hexadecimal values to their decimal equivalents.
  • fbsine2~ The fbsine2~ object is a non-interpolating sound generator that produces a feedback sine chaotic signal.
  • gcd The gcd object calculates the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two or more numbers.
  • plusequals~ The plusequals~ (or +=~) object in Pure Data continuously sums incoming audio signal values, maintaining a running total.
  • atan~ atan~ calculates the arc-tangent of an input signal, sample by sample.
  • gmean The gmean object generates a list of numbers representing a geometric progression between a specified start and end point.
  • midi2freq The midi2freq object converts MIDI note numbers (including fractional "MIDI cents") into corresponding frequencies in Hertz.
  • a.sum The a.sum object in Pure Data sums the last x numbers of an array.
  • kink~ The kink~ object distorts a phasor~ signal by applying a "kink" to its phase.
  • avg~ The avg~ object calculates the arithmetic mean of an incoming audio signal.
  • sum The sum object takes a list of numbers as its input.
  • clip The cyclone/clip object constrains incoming float or list values to a specified numerical range.
  • atanh~ The atanh~ object calculates the hyperbolic arc-tangent of an input signal, sample by sample.
  • crackle~ The crackle~ object generates noise using a chaotic difference equation.
  • samps2ms~ The samps2ms~ object converts a time value given in samples to milliseconds.
  • taint~ The taint~ object performs spectral multiplication, combining the frequency spectra of two input audio signals.
  • allpass~ The allpass~ object implements an all-pass filter, which passes all frequencies without altering their gain but changes their phase.
  • rescale~ The rescale~ object transforms incoming audio signals from a specified input range to a desired output range.
  • frac.add The frac.add object adds two fractional values together.
  • frac2dec The frac2dec object converts fractional values, including nested fractions and those with decimal numbers, into their decimal floating-point equivalents.
  • eqdiv The eqdiv object generates a musical scale by equally dividing a given interval into a specified number of divisions.
  • cosh The cosh object calculates the hyperbolic cosine of a given number.
  • rescale The rescale object maps input values from a specified input range to a new output range.
  • lcm The lcm object calculates the least common multiple (LCM) of two or more numbers.
  • quantize~ The quantize~ object quantizes an incoming audio signal to a variable number of steps.
  • rint~ The rint~ object rounds an incoming signal (float) to the nearest integer value.
  • minimum The minimum object outputs the smallest value from its inputs.
  • snapshot~ The cyclone/snapshot~ object converts a sample from an audio signal into a control-rate float.
  • ms2samps~ The ms2samps~ object converts time values from milliseconds to the corresponding number of audio samples, based on the current samplerate.
  • lin2db~ The lin2db~ object converts linear amplitude values to decibels full scale (dBFS).
  • cart2pol The cart2pol object converts 3D Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z) into 3D polar coordinates.
  • notedur2ratio The notedur2ratio object converts musical note duration symbols (e.g., 1n for whole note, 4n for quarter note) into their corresponding numerical ratios, which can be output as fractions or floats.
  • db2lin db2lin converts amplitude values from deciBel Full Scale (dBFS) to linear amplitude.
  • rand~ rand~ generates random values between -1 and 1 at a given frequency, linearly interpolating between these values to produce a bandlimited noise signal.
  • multiline~ The multiline~ object performs line-interpolated multiplication of multiple input signals.
  • rad2deg The rad2deg object converts radian values to degrees.
  • greaterthan~ The greaterthan~ (or >~) object in Pure Data performs a signal comparison.
  • scale2cents The scale2cents object converts a musical scale defined by semitone steps into cents values.
  • atanh The atanh object calculates the hyperbolic arc-tangent of a given number.
  • op~ The op~ object performs various signal-rate operations, including comparative (e.g., ==, >), logical (&&, ||, !), bitwise (&, |, ~, ^, <<, >>), and modulus (%) calculations.
  • minmax~ The minmax~ object tracks and outputs the minimum and maximum signal levels it has received since its creation or last reset.
  • cart2sph The cart2sph object converts 3D Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z) into spherical coordinates (radius, azimuth, elevation).
  • avg The avg object calculates the mean average of all numbers it has received.
  • modulo~ The modulo~ (or %~) object is a signal remainder operator.
  • e The e object outputs the mathematical constant 'e' (Euler's number).
  • bitshift~ bitshift~ performs bitwise shifting on an incoming signal, moving its bit values to the left or right.

docs.title: a.sum
docs.text:
The a.sum object in Pure Data sums the last x numbers of an array. It is useful for calculating averages of parameters or for smoothing signals to reduce noise.

    Open Pd and go to ToolsFind Externals. Search for neimog and install it. Then create an object with declare -lib neimog -path neimog. Finally, use a.sum or any other object from neimog.

docs.title: absgn~
docs.text:
The absgn~ object calculates the absolute value and the signum of an incoming audio signal. It takes a signal as input and outputs two signals: one for the absolute value and another for the signum. This object is part of the zexy library and is an abstraction, meaning an external version might offer better performance.

docs.title: abs~
docs.text:
The abs~ object calculates the absolute value of an incoming audio signal. It converts all negative signal values to their positive counterparts, effectively rectifying the signal. This is useful for ensuring signal values are always non-negative.

docs.title: coordinates
docs.text:
The cart2pol, pol2sph, sph2cart, cart2sph, pol2cart, and sph2pol objects convert between Cartesian, Polar, and Spherical coordinate systems. They take three float inputs and output three float values, with angles given in radians. These objects are fundamental for transforming spatial data representations.

docs.title: cosh
docs.text:
The cosh object calculates the hyperbolic cosine of a given number. It takes a float as input, which is stored, and outputs the hyperbolic cosine of that value. Sending a bang message to the object will trigger a recalculation and output based on the currently stored input.

docs.title: cosh~
docs.text:
The cosh~ object calculates the hyperbolic cosine of an input signal sample by sample. It is primarily used for mathematical computations on audio signals, outputting a signal representing the hyperbolic cosine of its input.

docs.title: cosx~
docs.text:
The cyclone/cosx~ object calculates the cosine of an incoming signal, expecting the input values to be in radians. It is designed for mathematical operations on audio signals, distinguishing it from the vanilla cos~ object which is often used with phasor~ for waveform generation.

docs.title: count~
docs.text:
The count~ object is a sample-accurate counter that outputs a signal incrementing by one for each elapsed sample. It can be configured with a minimum starting value and an optional maximum value for looping. The counter can be triggered and controlled by bangs, floats, or a signal input, and includes an @autoreset attribute.

docs.title: crackle~
docs.text:
The crackle~ object generates noise using a chaotic difference equation. Its behavior is controlled by a 'p' parameter (0 to 1), influencing the chaotic output. A clear message can be sent to reset the internal memory of the chaotic function.

docs.title: cross~
docs.text:
The cross~ object performs block convolution for cross-synthesis. It uses a threshold to determine when to apply the cross-synthesis process, allowing for dynamic blending of audio signals.

docs.title: cyclone
docs.text:
The cyclone library for Pure Data provides a comprehensive set of mathematical and comparative operators, many of which are direct aliases for common Max/MSP objects. It includes essential functions like equality (==~), inequality (!=~), less than (<~), greater than (>~), modulo (%~), and various arithmetic operations. This library is designed to offer familiar functionality and ease the transition for users accustomed to Max/MSP's object naming conventions.

docs.title: db2lin
docs.text:
db2lin converts amplitude values from deciBel Full Scale (dBFS) to linear amplitude. It uses the formula amp = pow(10, dBFS / 20). By default, a -100 dBFS input maps to zero linear amplitude, with the option to set a different minimum dB value.

docs.title: dbtoa
docs.text:
The dbtoa object converts decibel (dBFS) amplitude values to their corresponding linear amplitude. It performs this conversion using the mathematical expression amp = pow(10, dBFS / 20). This object is primarily used for translating logarithmic decibel values into linear amplitude values within audio processing contexts.

docs.text:

docs.title: delta~
docs.text:
The delta~ object calculates the difference between the current and previous sample of an incoming audio signal. It is useful for detecting changes or transients in a signal, effectively acting as a discrete-time differentiator.

docs.title: eqdiv
docs.text:
The eqdiv object generates a musical scale by equally dividing a given interval into a specified number of divisions. It outputs a list of ratios, either in cents or as direct ratios, representing the scale. This object is primarily used for creating custom temperaments and microtonal scales.

docs.title: fbsine2~
docs.text:
The fbsine2~ object is a non-interpolating sound generator that produces a feedback sine chaotic signal. Based on SuperCollider's 'FBSineN' UGEN, it uses difference equations to create complex waveforms. It accepts various parameters to control its chaotic behavior, including frequency and initial values for its internal states.

docs.title: floor
docs.text:
The floor object, part of the else library, performs a mathematical floor operation. It takes a floating-point number (or a list of numbers) and outputs the largest integer less than or equal to the input. This is useful for rounding down values in numerical calculations.

docs.title: fold
docs.text:
The fold object mirrors input values that exceed a defined range back into that range, effectively "folding" them. It accepts float or list inputs and can be configured with minimum and maximum boundaries via creation arguments or inlets. A bang outputs the last folded value, while a set message prepares the next value for folding.

docs.title: frameaccum~
docs.text:
frameaccum~ accumulates the values of each sample across incoming signal blocks, primarily designed for computing a running phase of FFT frames. It outputs a signal where each sample is the sum of corresponding samples from all previous input blocks. An optional argument allows wrapping the accumulated phase between -PI and PI.

docs.title: framedelta~
docs.text:
The framedelta~ object calculates the running phase deviation of an FFT analysis. It achieves this by subtracting the values of each sample in the previously received signal block from the corresponding samples in the current signal block. This object is specifically designed for use within an FFT subpatch where the FFT size matches the block size, and is often used in conjunction with phasewrap~ for accurate phase deviation tracking.

docs.text:

docs.title: function~
docs.text:
The function~ object generates arbitrary waveforms or functions from a list of points and periods. It takes a phase input (0 to 1) to read the defined function, making it suitable for creating custom oscillators or envelopes. It also supports defining the curvature of each segment and multichannel operation.

docs.text:

docs.text:

docs.title: gcd
docs.text:
The gcd object calculates the greatest common divisor (GCD) of two or more numbers. It accepts individual float inputs in its inlets, which are truncated to integers for the calculation. Alternatively, a list of numbers can be provided to the left inlet to find the GCD of multiple values.

docs.title: histogram
docs.text:
The histogram object records the frequency of incoming positive integer numbers (floats are truncated) into an internal table. It also specifically handles MIDI note-on messages, treating them as indices. Users can clear, resize, query, or export the histogram data, and optionally name or embed the internal table.

docs.title: hz2rad
docs.text:
The hz2rad object converts a frequency value in Hertz (Hz) to "Radians per Sample." This conversion is crucial for digital signal processing, as it calculates the phase increment per sample based on the patch's sample rate using the formula rad = (hz * 2*pi / sr).

AI Generated

This content was generated with the assistance of AI. If you notice any errors, please report them or submit a fix using Submit. Check the prompt used here.

    Open Pd and go to ToolsFind Externals. Search for else and install it. Then create an object with declare -lib else -path else. Finally, use hz2rad or any other object from else.

docs.title: iAmbient2D
docs.text:
The iAmbient2D object simulates ambient interactions in a 2D space, applying constant, random, and damping forces to named "masses." It also handles displacement if a mass moves outside a defined rectangular area. The object requires a bang to activate and its various force and position parameters can be configured via messages.

docs.title: ikeda~
docs.text:
The ikeda~ object is a chaotic signal generator based on the Ikeda map, producing two audio outputs. It calculates these outputs using a set of difference equations, allowing for dynamic and unpredictable signal generation. Users can control its behavior by setting the u parameter and initial values for its internal states.

docs.title: interpolate
docs.text:
The else/interpolate object performs smooth interpolation between a set of start and target values, which can be single numbers or lists. It takes an interpolation factor (typically 0 to 1) and can apply an exponential curve to the interpolation. This object is ideal for creating dynamic transitions and parameter automation.

docs.title: latoocarfian~
docs.text:
The latoocarfian~ object is a chaotic audio signal generator that implements the Latoocarfian attractor's difference equations. It produces a continuous audio signal whose behavior is determined by four coefficients (a, b, c, d) and initial x and y values. The output rate of the chaotic signal can be controlled in Hertz.

docs.title: lcm
docs.text:
The lcm object calculates the least common multiple (LCM) of two or more numbers. It truncates float inputs to integers for calculation, treating negative values and zero (as one) appropriately. The object can compute the LCM of two numbers via its inlets or a list of numbers provided to its left inlet.

docs.title: lin2db
docs.text:
The lin2db object converts a linear amplitude value to its equivalent decibel full scale (dBFS) representation. It performs the mathematical operation dbFS = log10(amp) * 20. This object is useful for translating linear amplitude measurements into a more perceptually relevant logarithmic scale.

docs.text:

docs.title: lincong~
docs.text:
The lincong~ object is a chaotic signal generator based on a linear congruential difference equation. It produces an audio signal scaled between -1 and 1, with its output rate controllable in Hertz. Parameters 'a', 'c', 'm', and the initial value can be set via a list of floats.

docs.title: linedrive
docs.text:
The linedrive object scales numbers from an input range to an output range using an exponential curve. It is designed to work with objects like line~ to create non-linear transitions, such as for gain changes. The exponential factor controls the steepness of the curve, with a value of 1 resulting in a linear scale.

docs.title: link
docs.text:
The link object calculates forces between two masses, simulating a physical connection with configurable rigidity and damping. It outputs the forces to be applied to each mass, enabling the creation of dynamic physical simulations. The object supports non-linear behaviors based on minimum and maximum link lengths, allowing for attractive or repulsive forces.

docs.title: lorenz~
docs.text:
The lorenz~ object is a chaotic signal generator based on the Lorenz strange attractor, a mathematical model of atmospheric convection. It outputs the 'x' dimension of the system's state as an audio signal. Users can control its behavior by setting the frequency and the Lorenz system's 's', 'r', 'b', and 'h' coefficients, as well as initial x, y, and z values.

docs.title: mag
docs.text:
The mag object calculates the magnitude (amplitude) from Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts), which is fundamental for analyzing frequency spectrum data. It can also compute the power magnitude (sum of squares) if given the "power" argument, providing a versatile tool for spectral analysis.

docs.title: mag~
docs.text:
The mag~ object calculates the magnitude (amplitude) or power magnitude of a complex signal from its real and imaginary parts. It takes real and imaginary components as inputs and outputs the corresponding magnitude, similar to the amplitude output of car2pol~. This object is particularly useful for spectral processing where phase information is not required, and it supports multichannel audio.

docs.title: markov
docs.text:
The markov object in Pure Data generates sequences based on Markov chains. It learns patterns from input data (floats, symbols, or lists) and can generate new sequences of varying "order," influencing the randomness of the output. It supports state saving and reproducible sequences via seeding.

docs.title: mass3D
docs.text:
The mass3D object simulates a mass in a 3D space, calculating its position and velocity based on applied forces, weight, damping, and boundary conditions. It outputs the mass's current X, Y, Z position, velocity, and total applied force, making it suitable for physical modeling and interactive simulations.

docs.text:

docs.text:

It provides these values both as audio signals and as floats, which can be triggered by a bang. This object is useful for analyzing the dynamic range of an audio signal over time.

docs.text:

docs.title: modulo~
docs.text:

docs.title: morph
docs.text:
The morph object is an abstraction that extends the functionality of Pure Data's line object to lists of numbers. It allows for smooth transitions (morphing) between different lists over a specified time, with configurable grain and an optional exponential curve. It dynamically handles varying list sizes, either forgetting extra elements or initializing new ones to zero.

docs.title: ms2samps
docs.text:
The ms2samps object is a simple utility that converts a time value in milliseconds to the equivalent number of audio samples. It uses the formula samps = ms * samplerate/1000 for this conversion. This object is useful for precisely timing events or durations within Pure Data patches based on the current audio sample rate.

docs.text:
The mstosamps~ object converts a time value in milliseconds to the corresponding number of audio samples, taking the current sample rate into account. It accepts both float and signal inputs for the millisecond value. A signal input will output a signal, while a float input provides both float and signal outputs, with the signal input taking precedence if both are present.

docs.title: pi
docs.text:
The pi object calculates and outputs the mathematical constant Pi. It can take an optional float argument or a second inlet to multiply the value of Pi. The object outputs the result when a patch is loaded or when it receives a bang message, and can store the value in a named variable.

docs.title: plusequals~
docs.text:
The plusequals~ (or +=~) object in Pure Data continuously sums incoming audio signal values, maintaining a running total. This signal accumulator can be reset to zero by a bang message or a non-zero signal in its right inlet, and its initial sum can be set via a set <float> message or an argument.

docs.title: pmpd2d
docs.text:
The pmpd2d object implements a 2D mass-spring-damper physical model, designed for particle-based simulations in Pure Data. It allows users to define and manipulate 'masses' (particles with mass, position, and mobility) and 'links' (connections between masses with properties like rigidity, damping, and length). The object calculates forces and updates the state of the physical system, providing data for external visualization or control.

docs.title: pol2car
docs.text:
The pol2car object converts polar coordinates (amplitude and phase) into Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts). It takes amplitude in its left inlet and phase in radians in its right inlet, outputting the real part from its left outlet and the imaginary part from its right outlet. This object is useful for mathematical transformations between coordinate systems, often applied in graphics, spatial audio, or complex number operations.

docs.title: pol2cart
docs.text:
The pol2cart object converts 3D polar coordinates (radius r, azimuthal angle phi, and polar angle theta) into Cartesian coordinates (x, y, z). It takes three float inlets for r, phi, and theta (all angles in radians) and outputs the corresponding x, y, and z float values from its three outlets. This object is useful for transforming spatial data representations.

docs.text:

It is primarily used in spectral processing workflows, allowing for manipulation of frequency domain data in a more intuitive polar form after an FFT and before an inverse FFT.

docs.title: pol2sph
docs.text:
The pol2sph object converts 2D polar coordinates (radius, angle, and height) into 3D spherical coordinates. It takes three float inlets for r (radius), phi (angle in radians), and z (height), and outputs the corresponding spherical coordinates as three floats. This object is useful for transformations between different coordinate systems, particularly in control applications.

docs.title: poltocar~
docs.text:
The poltocar~ object converts signal values from polar coordinates (amplitude and phase) to Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts). It is primarily used in spectral processing workflows, often after an FFT, to transform amplitude and phase data back into a format suitable for an inverse FFT. This allows for manipulation of spectral data in a more intuitive polar form.

docs.title: polyfun
docs.text:
The polyfun object evaluates a polynomial function. It takes a float as the variable 'x' in its left inlet and a list of coefficients (a0 to aN) in its right inlet, with the list's length determining the polynomial's order. This allows for dynamic calculation of various polynomial expressions, such as y=2x+1 or y=3-x^2.

docs.title: pong
docs.text:
The pong object in Pure Data is a utility for manipulating numerical inputs. It can fold, wrap, or clip incoming numbers or lists of numbers within a specified low-high range. The wrap mode is particularly useful for modulo arithmetic operations.

docs.title: power~
docs.text:
power~ is a signal processing object in Pure Data that acts as a power function waveshaper. It raises an input signal to a specified exponential factor, extending standard exponentiation to correctly handle negative signal inputs by returning -pow(-a, b). This allows for the exponentiation of negative values by non-integer exponents, making it useful for various signal transformations.

docs.title: pz2coeff
docs.text:

docs.title: quad~
docs.text:
The quad~ object generates a chaotic audio signal based on a quadratic difference equation: y[n] = a * y[n-1]^2 + b * y[n-1] + c. It allows control over the signal's output rate (frequency in Hz) and the coefficients a, b, c, as well as the initial value of y[n-1].

docs.title: rad2hz
docs.text:
The rad2hz object converts a frequency value from "Radians per Sample" to Hertz. This conversion is dependent on the current patch's sample rate (sr) and uses the formula: hz = rad * sr / (2 * pi). It is primarily used for accurate frequency calculations within audio signal processing.

docs.title: ratio2cents
docs.text:
The ratio2cents object converts rational number intervals (expressed as floating-point decimal values) into cents. It uses the formula cents = log2(rational) * 1200 to perform this mathematical conversion, providing a way to represent musical intervals in a standardized unit.

docs.title: rdiv
docs.text:
The rdiv (or !/) object performs division with reversed inlet behavior compared to the standard / object. It divides the value received in its right (cold) inlet by the value received in its left (hot) inlet. This object is useful when the divisor is the incoming message.

docs.title: rdiv~
docs.text:

Unlike the standard /~ object, rdiv~ reverses the inlet functions, dividing a number (from the right inlet) by the incoming signal value on the left inlet. It is useful for specific signal processing scenarios where this reversed division order is required.

docs.title: rescale
docs.text:
The rescale object maps input values from a specified input range to a new output range. It supports linear, exponential, logarithmic, and reverse-logarithmic scaling, allowing for flexible value transformations. Users can also control whether values outside the defined input range are clipped or scaled proportionally.

docs.text:
The samps2ms object is a simple abstraction that converts a time value given in audio samples into milliseconds. It performs this conversion using the formula: ms = 1000 * samples / samplerate. This is useful for translating sample-based durations into a more human-readable time unit.

docs.title: scalarmult
docs.text:
The scalarmult object performs scalar multiplication, also known as a "dot product," on incoming lists of numbers. It takes two lists as input and outputs a single list containing the result. It can also multiply a list by a single float value, effectively casting the float to a list for the operation.

It uses a base pitch (defaulting to MIDI 60) and a frequency range (defaulting to 20-20000 Hz) to generate the output. This object is useful for preparing frequency data to drive other audio synthesis or processing modules.

docs.title: shaper~
docs.text:
The shaper~ object performs waveshaping by applying a user-defined transfer function to an input signal. This function can be an external array or an internal Chebyshev polynomial, whose harmonic weights can be set via arguments or messages. It also features an optional internal high-pass filter and can be used for advanced sample manipulation, such as creating turntable scratch effects.

docs.title: sum~
docs.text:
The sum~ object takes a multi-channel audio signal and sums all its channels into a single mono output signal. By default, it performs this summing operation; however, if a zero is sent to its control inlet, the multi-channel signal passes through unchanged. This object is useful for converting multi-channel audio streams into a mono stream or for conditional summing.

docs.text:
The tCylinder3D object defines a 3D cylindrical volume and tests if a given 3D point (referred to as a "mass") is located within its boundaries. It outputs a boolean (0 or 1) indicating containment, along with the distance and speed of the mass relative to the cylinder's center. Parameters allow setting the cylinder's orientation, center, and its inner/outer radii and height limits.

docs.title: tLine2D
docs.text:
The tLine2D object defines a 2D line segment using two points and tracks the position of a "mass" relative to it. It outputs the mass's distance from the segment, a related "speed" value, and a binary (0 or 1) indicator based on the mass's position. This object is primarily used for testing and interacting with 2D mass positions within a defined linear boundary.

docs.title: tabgen
docs.text:
tabgen is a Pure Data abstraction that generates various mathematical functions (e.g., sine, Gaussian, sinc, trapezoid, variable sawtooth, noise, and FFT window functions) and populates a specified table (array) with the generated data. It can also apply these functions as envelopes to existing values within a table. The object allows for initial table sizing and setting the table name.

docs.title: wrap
docs.text:
The wrap object functions as a float-capable modulo, constraining an input float value within specified limits. It can operate with no arguments (wrapping around 0), one argument (wrapping between 0 and the argument), or two arguments (wrapping between a lower and upper limit). The limits can also be set dynamically via its second inlet, with wrapping always inclusive of the lower limit and exclusive of the upper.

docs.title: MIDI
docs.text:
• keyboard The keyboard object provides a graphical piano keyboard interface for Pure Data.
  • midi The midi object in Pure Data is a versatile tool for playing, recording, and manipulating raw MIDI data streams.
  • borax The borax object processes incoming MIDI note and velocity data, reporting detailed information about active notes.
  • pgm.in The pgm.in object extracts MIDI Program Change messages (0-127) from either a connected MIDI device or an external raw MIDI data stream.
  • retune The retune object remaps incoming MIDI pitches to a custom scale.
  • sfont.m~ The sfont.m~ object is a convenient wrapper around sfont~ designed for soundfont-based synthesis.
  • pluck~ The pluck~ object implements the Karplus-Strong algorithm for physical modeling synthesis, simulating plucked string sounds.
  • xbendin The xbendin object retrieves 14-bit pitch bend messages from raw MIDI input data.
  • touch.in The touch.in object extracts MIDI Aftertouch (channel pressure) information from either a connected MIDI device or an external raw MIDI data stream.
  • scales The scales object generates musical scales based on a list of semitone intervals, outputting them as note names or MIDI pitches.
  • midi.in The midi.in object processes incoming MIDI data, converting raw MIDI streams into "cooked" data with type symbols, values, and channel information.
  • impulse~ The impulse~ object is an impulse oscillator capable of generating signals with negative frequencies.
  • midi.out The midi.out object sends MIDI messages to an external MIDI device, converting "cooked" MIDI data (e.g., ctl, note, pgm) into raw MIDI bytes.
  • pgm.out The pgm.out object formats and sends raw MIDI program change messages (values 0-127) to Pure Data's MIDI output and its outlet.
  • bend.in The bend.in object extracts MIDI Pitch Bend messages from connected MIDI devices or raw MIDI data streams.
  • note.out The note.out object formats and sends MIDI note messages, including Note On and Note Off (with optional release velocity), to Pure Data's MIDI output or its own outlet.
  • sfont~ sfont~ is a sampler synthesizer that plays SoundFont (.sf2/.sf3) files, based on FluidLite.
  • panic The panic object acts as a "panic button" for MIDI notes.
  • sustain The sustain object emulates a MIDI sustain pedal, holding note-off messages while active and releasing them when deactivated.
  • sysrt.out The sysrt.out object formats and sends raw MIDI system real-time messages.
  • xbendin2 The xbendin2 object processes incoming raw MIDI data to extract the Most Significant Byte (MSB) and Least Significant Byte (LSB) of pitch bend messages.
  • xbendout2 The xbendout2 object formats 14-bit pitch bend messages into two 7-bit values (MSB and LSB) and sends them as a raw MIDI data stream.
  • mono~ The mono~ object acts as a monophonic signal voice manager, emulating monophonic synthesizer behavior.
  • ctl.out The ctl.out object formats and sends raw MIDI control messages.
  • xbendout The xbendout object formats and sends 14-bit MIDI pitch bend messages.
  • noteinfo The noteinfo object tracks and outputs information about active MIDI notes.
  • bl.saw~ The bl.saw~ object generates a bandlimited, anti-aliased sawtooth waveform, suitable for high-quality audio synthesis.
  • flush The flush object functions as a "panic button" for MIDI Note-on messages.
  • midiparse The midiparse object receives raw MIDI data, typically from midiin, and parses it into various MIDI messages.
  • makenote2 The makenote2 object converts a list of pitch, velocity, and duration into MIDI note-on and note-off messages.
  • seq8.m~ The seq8.m~ object is a simple 8-step sequencer for Pure Data, designed to generate musical sequences.
  • sendmidi The sendmidi object is a helper abstraction designed to send MIDI messages to the pd~ object.
  • gaussian~ The gaussian~ object is a Gaussian oscillator that generates a bell-shaped waveform.
  • abs.pd~ The abs.pd~ object loads a Pure Data patch as a subprocess, making it easier to manage complex patches.
  • seq The seq object in Pure Data is a MIDI sequencer that can play, record, load, and save MIDI streams to and from MIDI or text files.
  • midiflush The midiflush object acts as a "panic button" for MIDI notes.
  • voices~ The voices~ object is a polyphonic voice allocator that generates multichannel signals for controlling pitch and gate.
  • ptouch.out The ptouch.out object formats and sends raw MIDI polyphonic aftertouch messages.
  • midi.clock The midi.clock object abstracts MIDI clock functionality, allowing synchronization with either an internal clock (set by a float input) or an external MIDI device.
  • synth~ synth~ is a Pure Data object that serves as a wrapper for loading and managing monophonic or polyphonic synthesizer abstractions.
  • xnotein The xnotein object parses raw MIDI data streams, providing more detailed information than the standard notein object.
  • square~ square~ is a versatile square wave oscillator that generates audio signals with adjustable pulse width, initial phase, and phase modulation.
  • ctl.in The ctl.in object extracts MIDI Control Change (CC) messages from either a connected MIDI device or an external raw MIDI data stream.
  • midiformat The midiformat object converts various discrete MIDI inputs, such as note, control change, and pitch bend messages, into a single raw MIDI message stream.
  • plaits.m~ The plaits.m~ object is a versatile polyphonic sound synthesis module, wrapping the [plaits~] external.
  • midi2note The midi2note object converts MIDI pitch values into musical note names, such as 'Eb3', including an octave number where middle C (MIDI pitch 60) is C4.
  • rec2 The rec2 object records incoming MIDI note messages (pitch and velocity) into a [text] object.
  • pluck.m~ The pluck.m~ object is a polyphonic physical modeling synthesizer for plucked string sounds, acting as a wrapper around the pluck~ object from the ELSE library.
  • note.in The note.in object extracts MIDI pitch and velocity information from incoming MIDI data streams.
  • gain~ The gain~ object provides mono and multichannel gain control for audio signals.
  • freq2midi The freq2midi object converts frequency values (Hz) to MIDI note numbers, similar to ftom.
  • suspedal The suspedal object emulates a MIDI sustain pedal, holding Note Off messages when active and releasing them when deactivated.
  • mpe.in The mpe.in object processes MIDI Polyphonic Expression (MPE) data, converting raw MIDI input from either an internal device or an external source into structured MPE messages.
  • gain2~ The gain2~ object is a stereo gain abstraction for Pure Data, designed to adjust the gain of stereo audio signals.
  • sfz~ The sfz~ object is a Pure Data synthesizer that plays SFZ instruments, which are text-based definitions for sample-based and hybrid synthesis.
  • midi2freq The midi2freq object converts MIDI note numbers (including fractional "MIDI cents") into corresponding frequencies in Hertz.
  • vco.m~ The vco.m~ object is a polyphonic voltage-controlled oscillator for Pure Data, offering various waveforms including sine, triangle, saw, variable saw, and square.
  • voices The voices object manages voice allocation for polyphonic synthesizers, offering advanced features beyond poly.
  • touch.out The touch.out object formats and sends MIDI aftertouch messages.
  • midi.learn The midi.learn object is a Pure Data abstraction designed to learn and store incoming MIDI data, such as control changes, program changes, notes, and bends.
  • ptouch.in ptouch.in extracts MIDI polyphonic aftertouch messages from raw MIDI input, such as from a connected MIDI device or an external MIDI data stream.
  • sysrt.in The sysrt.in object detects and parses System and real-time MIDI messages from an incoming raw MIDI data stream.
  • keymap The keymap object translates computer keyboard presses into MIDI note on/off messages, effectively turning your QWERTY keyboard into a musical input device.
  • xnoteout The xnoteout object sends MIDI note messages, offering enhanced control over the standard noteout object by including both Note On and Note Off (release) velocities.
  • note2midi The note2midi object converts musical note names (e.g., 'C4', 'E#+5', 'Db-4') into their corresponding MIDI pitch values.
  • bend.out The bend.out object formats and sends MIDI pitch bend messages.
  • autotune The autotune object retunes incoming MIDI pitches to the closest step within a specified musical scale.

docs.title: autotune
docs.text:
The autotune object retunes incoming MIDI pitches to the closest step within a specified musical scale. It takes a list of cents values defining the scale and a base MIDI pitch, allowing for custom microtonal or alternative tunings. It outputs the retuned MIDI pitch.

docs.title: bend.in
docs.text:
The bend.in object extracts MIDI Pitch Bend messages from connected MIDI devices or raw MIDI data streams. It outputs normalized float values from -1 to 1 by default, or raw integer values from 0 to 16383 when in raw mode. It can filter by MIDI channel or operate in omni mode, also supporting external MIDI input.

docs.title: bend.out
docs.text:
The bend.out object formats and sends MIDI pitch bend messages. It takes normalized float values (from -1 to 1) by default, or raw 0-16383 values with the -raw flag, and outputs them to Pd's MIDI output and its outlet. It can also be configured to output only to its outlet using the -ext flag or ext message, allowing for flexible routing of pitch bend data.

docs.title: borax
docs.text:
The borax object processes incoming MIDI note and velocity data, reporting detailed information about active notes. It outputs parameters such as note event number, voice number, number of active voices, pitch, velocity, note duration, and delta-time between Note-On events. This object is particularly useful for tracking and routing individual MIDI voices.

It can filter messages by MIDI channel and/or control number, outputting the control value, control number, and MIDI channel. It supports an "omni" mode to receive all channels and an "external input only" mode.

docs.title: flush
docs.text:
The flush object functions as a "panic button" for MIDI Note-on messages. It tracks Note-on messages that have not been switched off and, when triggered by a bang, sends corresponding Note-off messages for all currently hanging notes. It also supports setting pitch/velocity and clearing its internal state.

docs.title: gain2~
docs.text:
The gain2~ object is a stereo gain abstraction for Pure Data, designed to adjust the gain of stereo audio signals. It offers flexible control over gain scaling (quartic, dB, or linear), allows setting a maximum gain, and includes a configurable ramp time for smooth transitions. It also supports MIDI learning for external control and can handle multichannel audio input.

It supports negative frequencies and features inlets for controlling width, phase synchronization, and phase modulation. Additionally, it offers multichannel audio support and can accept frequency input in either Hertz or MIDI pitch.

docs.text:

It outputs MIDI note and velocity values based on mouse clicks and drags, and can be configured with various parameters like octave range, key width, and toggle behavior. It also supports programmatic control of notes via messages like on, off, play, and show.

docs.title: makenote2
docs.text:
The makenote2 object converts a list of pitch, velocity, and duration into MIDI note-on and note-off messages. It supports symbolic pitches (e.g., 'C4') and allows additional data in the input list, which can be passed to the note-off message. Duration is specified in beats, with the BPM configurable via an argument or inlet, allowing for millisecond durations if BPM is set to 60.000.

docs.text:
The midi object in Pure Data is a versatile tool for playing, recording, and manipulating raw MIDI data streams. It supports reading from and writing to MIDI files, as well as importing and exporting MIDI data to text files. It can also synchronize with external clock sources and provides control messages for playback, recording, and data management.

docs.title: midi.out
docs.text:

Unlike midiout, it handles various MIDI message types directly. It can also output raw MIDI data to its outlet, optionally exclusively via the -ext flag or ext message.

docs.text:

docs.title: midiformat
docs.text:
The midiformat object converts various discrete MIDI inputs, such as note, control change, and pitch bend messages, into a single raw MIDI message stream. It serves as the counterpart to midiparse, enabling the construction of complete MIDI messages from individual components. This object is essential for preparing structured MIDI data for output or further processing.

docs.title: midiparse
docs.text:
The midiparse object receives raw MIDI data, typically from midiin, and parses it into various MIDI messages. It outputs note messages (pitch/velocity), polyphonic aftertouch, control change (value/controller number), program change, channel aftertouch, and pitch bend messages, along with the MIDI channel. It also supports a @hires attribute for setting pitch bend precision.

docs.title: note2midi
docs.text:
The note2midi object converts musical note names (e.g., 'C4', 'E#+5', 'Db-4') into their corresponding MIDI pitch values. It supports enharmonic spellings, double sharps/flats (using 'x' and 'bb'), and quarter tones (using '+' and '-'). The object expects note names as symbols or lists, with 'C4' representing MIDI pitch 60, and handles an octave range from 0 to 8.

docs.title: noteinfo
docs.text:
The noteinfo object tracks and outputs information about active MIDI notes. It provides the number of currently active voices from its right outlet and a detailed list (voice number, event number, pitch, velocity, duration) for each note event from its left outlet. Sending a bang to its right inlet resets its internal memory, sending note-offs for all active notes.

docs.title: ptouch.in
docs.text:
ptouch.in extracts MIDI polyphonic aftertouch messages from raw MIDI input, such as from a connected MIDI device or an external MIDI data stream. It outputs the aftertouch value, the key, and the MIDI channel from which the message originated. The object can be configured to listen to a specific MIDI channel or operate in omni mode.

docs.title: ptouch.out
docs.text:
The ptouch.out object formats and sends raw MIDI polyphonic aftertouch messages. It takes key, pressure, and an optional channel number as input, outputting the MIDI stream to Pd's MIDI output and its outlet. It can also be configured to output only to its outlet using the -ext flag or ext message.

docs.title: rec2
docs.text:
The rec2 object records incoming MIDI note messages (pitch and velocity) into a [text] object. This recorded data can then be used by a [text sequence] object for playback, effectively capturing and storing MIDI sequences.

docs.title: retune
docs.text:
The retune object remaps incoming MIDI pitches to a custom scale. It takes a list of steps in cents and a base MIDI pitch, then retunes subsequent MIDI pitch inputs relative to this base and scale. This allows for microtonal or alternative tunings.

docs.title: scales
docs.text:
The scales object generates musical scales based on a list of semitone intervals, outputting them as note names or MIDI pitches. It allows specifying a starting note, octave, and can be configured to output MIDI pitch values using the -pitch flag.

docs.title: sendmidi
docs.text:
The sendmidi object is a helper abstraction designed to send MIDI messages to the pd~ object. This is necessary because pd~ cannot directly receive MIDI input from connected devices in Pure Data. It acts as a bridge, allowing MIDI data to be passed into subpatches managed by pd~.

docs.title: sfont~
docs.text:
sfont~ is a sampler synthesizer that plays SoundFont (.sf2/.sf3) files, based on FluidLite. It provides stereo audio output and responds to various MIDI messages for note playback, program/bank changes, and continuous controllers. The object also features advanced microtonal capabilities, allowing for custom scales and pitch remapping.

docs.title: sfz~
docs.text:
The sfz~ object is a Pure Data synthesizer that plays SFZ instruments, which are text-based definitions for sample-based and hybrid synthesis. It interprets .sfz files to arrange and play samples, and can also generate sounds using internal oscillators and apply effects like filters and envelopes. It supports extensive MIDI control for performance and microtonal tuning.

docs.title: square~
docs.text:
square~ is a versatile square wave oscillator that generates audio signals with adjustable pulse width, initial phase, and phase modulation. It supports negative frequencies, multichannel operation, and can be reset by an impulse signal. Frequency input can be set to hertz or MIDI pitch, and it offers both hard and soft sync modes.

docs.title: suspedal
docs.text:
The suspedal object emulates a MIDI sustain pedal, holding Note Off messages when active and releasing them when deactivated. It supports two sustain modes: a default 'regular' mode and a 'tonal' mode, which sustains currently playing notes without affecting new ones, similar to a piano's sostenuto pedal. It also offers various retrigger behaviors for repeated notes.

docs.text:

docs.text:
synth~ is a Pure Data object that serves as a wrapper for loading and managing monophonic or polyphonic synthesizer abstractions. It simplifies synth creation by handling voice allocation, MIDI note input, and multichannel audio output, while also allowing custom messages to be passed to the loaded synth abstraction.

docs.title: sysrt.in
docs.text:
The sysrt.in object detects and parses System and real-time MIDI messages from an incoming raw MIDI data stream. It outputs bangs for specific MIDI events like clock, tick, start, continue, stop, active sensing, and reset, making it useful for synchronizing and controlling processes based on MIDI timing and status messages.

docs.title: touch.in
docs.text:
The touch.in object extracts MIDI Aftertouch (channel pressure) information from either a connected MIDI device or an external raw MIDI data stream. It outputs the Aftertouch value and the corresponding MIDI channel. It can be configured to listen to all MIDI channels (omni mode) or a specific channel, and supports multiple MIDI ports by encoding the port number within the channel number.

docs.title: vco.m~
docs.text:
The vco.m~ object is a polyphonic voltage-controlled oscillator for Pure Data, offering various waveforms including sine, triangle, saw, variable saw, and square. It supports linear frequency modulation (FM), phase modulation (PM), pulse width modulation (PWM), and soft sync, with attenuverters for modulation inputs. It accepts MIDI pitch input and outputs audio signals.

docs.text:
The voices~ object is a polyphonic voice allocator that generates multichannel signals for controlling pitch and gate. It takes MIDI note messages as input and outputs corresponding pitch values (in MIDI) and normalized gate values (0-1) for each active voice, enabling polyphonic synthesis.

docs.title: xbendin
docs.text:
The xbendin object retrieves 14-bit pitch bend messages from raw MIDI input data. It outputs the 14-bit pitch bend value (0-16383) and optionally the MIDI channel. This object is considered redundant and deprecated, with bendin or cyclone/midiparse being preferred alternatives.

docs.title: xbendin2
docs.text:
The xbendin2 object processes incoming raw MIDI data to extract the Most Significant Byte (MSB) and Least Significant Byte (LSB) of pitch bend messages. These 7-bit values can be combined to form a 14-bit pitch bend value. It also outputs the MIDI channel number if not explicitly set. Note that this object is considered redundant; users are advised to use bendin or else/bend.in instead.

docs.title: xnotein
docs.text:
The xnotein object parses raw MIDI data streams, providing more detailed information than the standard notein object. It outputs the MIDI note number, the velocity for both Note On and Note Off messages, and a state indicator for Note On (1) or Note Off (0). This object is particularly useful for capturing release velocity from MIDI devices.
