docs.title: Multichannel
docs.text:
• out~ out~ is a convenient Pure Data abstraction for managing audio output.
  • quantizer~ The quantizer~ object quantizes an incoming audio signal to discrete step values, which can be defined by an argument or a second inlet.
  • unmerge~ unmerge~ separates the channels of a multichannel signal into groups of a specified size.
  • xgate~ The xgate~ object routes an incoming audio signal to one of its multiple outlets, supporting up to 500 channels.
  • out8~ The out8~ object is an octaphonic (8-channel) audio output abstraction for Pure Data.
  • tabplayer~ tabplayer~ is a multichannel array player for Pure Data, designed for flexible audio playback from tables.
  • wt2d~ The wt2d~ object is a 2-dimensional wavetable oscillator that reads from a Pure Data array.
  • drive.m~ drive.m~ is a multichannel audio object that applies a distortion or overdrive effect to incoming audio signals.
  • trunc~ The trunc~ object truncates an audio signal towards zero, effectively taking only the integer part of each sample's value.
  • car2pol~ The car2pol~ object converts a signal from Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts) to polar coordinates (amplitude and phase).
  • xgate2~ xgate2~ routes a single input signal to a specified number of output channels, applying crossfading between adjacent channels.
  • white~ The white~ object generates white noise using a pseudo-random number generator.
  • smooth~ The smooth~ object provides a signal smoothing function, transitioning input signals with linear or exponential interpolation over a specified time in milliseconds.
  • crusher.m~ The crusher.m~ object is a multichannel audio effect that wraps the crusher~ object from the ELSE library.
  • cosine~ cosine~ is a versatile cosine oscillator that generates audio signals.
  • plate.rev~ The plate.rev~ object is a Pure Data abstraction that implements a stereo plate reverb audio effect.
  • envgen~ envgen~ is a versatile audio envelope and line generator that creates complex amplitude curves based on lists of duration and target values.
  • slew~ The slew~ object limits the rate of change of an incoming signal, smoothing transitions between values.
  • free.rev~ The free.rev~ object implements a stereo reverb effect based on the Freeverb algorithm, utilizing parallel comb filters and series allpass filters.
  • bl.tri~ The bl.tri~ object is a bandlimited (anti-aliased) triangle wave oscillator designed for audio synthesis.
  • bl.imp2~ bl.imp2~ is a bandlimited, two-sided impulse oscillator that generates an audio signal.
  • floor~ The floor~ object performs a floor mathematical operation on incoming audio signals, rounding each sample down to the nearest integer.
  • float2sig~ The float2sig~ (or f2s~) object converts control-rate floats or lists of floats into audio-rate signals.
  • impulse~ The impulse~ object is an impulse oscillator capable of generating signals with negative frequencies.
  • wave~ The wave~ object reads audio data from a specified array (wavetable) using a phase signal (0-1) as an index.
  • sf-play_record The sf-play_record object provides a hard disk recording and playback system for Pure Data, enabling multi-track audio recordings.
  • gray~ The gray~ object generates a unique type of noise based on "gray code" (reflected binary code), which results from flipping random bits using a pseudo-random number generator.
  • vocoder~ The vocoder~ object is a classic channel vocoder used for cross-synthesis.
  • mono.rev~ The mono.rev~ object is a reverb abstraction that takes a mono audio input and produces a stereo audio output.
  • tabreader~ The tabreader~ object reads signal data from a Pure Data array (table).
  • resonbank~ resonbank~ is a bank of resonators designed for sound synthesis, built upon resonator~ objects.
  • index~ The index~ object reads values from an array without interpolation, using a signal as the index to read the array.
  • stereo.rev~ stereo.rev~ is a stereo input/output reverb abstraction, providing two independent reverb channels.
  • autofade.mc~ The autofade.mc~ object provides automatic fade-in and fade-out functionality for multichannel audio signals.
  • vsaw~ vsaw~ is a versatile audio oscillator that generates variable sawtooth and triangle waveforms.
  • dust~ dust~ generates random positive impulses at a specified density, based on SuperCollider's "Dust" UGEN.
  • autofade2.mc~ The autofade2.mc~ object provides automatic fade-in and fade-out functionality for multichannel audio signals.
  • pan~ pan~ performs circular or azimuth panning of an input signal across a user-defined number of output channels.
  • pmpd~ The pmpd~ object is a 1D particle-based physical modeling external for audio synthesis in Pure Data.
  • pan4~ The pan4~ object is a 4-channel equal power (sine/cosine) panner designed for quadraphonic audio.
  • trig.delay~ The trig.delay~ object delays an impulse signal by a specified time.
  • pm4~ The pm4~ object is a 4-operator phase modulation (FM) synthesizer.
  • delace~ The delace~ object deinterleaves a multichannel audio signal, splitting it into multiple separate signal outlets.
  • xgate.mc~ The xgate.mc~ object routes a multichannel audio input to a selected multichannel output.
  • nchs~ The nchs~ object queries the number of channels in a multi-channel signal connection, such as those provided by snake~ in or clone.
  • mag~ The mag~ object calculates the magnitude (amplitude) or power magnitude of a complex signal from its real and imaginary parts.
  • tabwriter~ The tabwriter~ object records incoming audio signals into Pure Data arrays.
  • parabolic~ parabolic~ is a parabolic oscillator that generates a non-band-limited waveform, similar to a sinusoid but with aliasing.
  • rotate~ The rotate~ object performs equal power rotation across 'n' audio channels, with a default of 2.
  • matrix~ The matrix~ object routes and mixes audio signals from multiple inlets to multiple outlets.
  • slew2~ The slew2~ object provides independent slew limiting for upward and downward signal ramps.
  • wavetable~ wavetable~ is an interpolating wavetable oscillator that reads audio data from a Pure Data array.
  • scope3d~ The scope3d~ object is an experimental 3D oscilloscope display for Pure Data.
  • autofade2~ autofade2~ is a Pure Data object that provides automatic fade-in and fade-out functionality for multiple audio inputs.
  • oscbank~ The oscbank~ object generates complex audio signals by combining multiple sine~ oscillators, allowing users to define a list of partials, amplitudes, and phases for each.
  • rand.f~ The rand.f~ object generates random floating-point values within a specified range.
  • sigs~ The sigs~ object generates constant multichannel audio signals from a list of numerical arguments.
  • spread.mc~ The spread.mc~ object distributes multichannel audio input across a specified number of output channels using equal power panning.
  • bl.imp~ bl.imp~ is a bandlimited impulse oscillator that generates an impulse signal at a specified frequency.
  • asr~ The asr~ object generates an attack/sustain/release envelope, acting as a simplified version of adsr~.
  • fold~ The fold~ object performs wavefolding, a waveshaping technique that mirrors audio signals back into a specified low and high range.
  • smooth2~ The smooth2~ object provides signal smoothing with independent ramp-up and ramp-down times, useful for creating portamento or glide effects between signal values.
  • pm2~ The pm2~ object is a 2-operator phase modulation (PM) synthesizer.
  • pm6~ The pm6~ object is a 6-operator phase modulation (FM) synthesizer.
  • balance~ The balance~ object performs equal-power (sin/cos) stereo balancing.
  • bl.saw~ The bl.saw~ object generates a bandlimited, anti-aliased sawtooth waveform, suitable for high-quality audio synthesis.
  • gatedelay~ The gatedelay~ object delays the "on" state of an incoming gate signal by a specified time, while immediately passing through the "off" state.
  • meter8~ The meter8~ object provides an octaphonic (8-channel) VU-meter, displaying the RMS/peak amplitude of incoming audio signals.
  • xfade~ The xfade~ object crossfades between two audio sources, each supporting 1 to 64 channels.
  • function~ The function~ object generates arbitrary waveforms or functions from a list of points and periods.
  • lag~ The lag~ object is a one-pole audio filter that applies an exponential glide or portamento to changes in its signal input.
  • sin~ The sin~ object computes the sine of its signal input, scaled by two pi, outputting the result as a signal.
  • adsr~ adsr~ is an audio-rate Attack, Decay, Sustain, Release envelope generator.
  • ping.pong~ The ping.pong~ object is an audio effect that creates a ping-pong delay.
  • out4~ out4~ is a quadraphonic audio output abstraction for Pure Data, providing click-free control over DSP on/off, mute, and gain.
  • buffer~ The buffer~ object stores audio data in memory as a multichannel array, supporting up to 64 channels.
  • ceil~ The ceil~ object is a signal-rate mathematical function that performs the ceiling operation on incoming audio signals.
  • sample~ The sample~ object creates and manages an audio buffer, enabling users to load, record, save, and manipulate audio samples.
  • db2lin~ The db2lin~ object converts decibel Full Scale (dBFS) amplitude values to linear amplitude values.
  • impulse2~ impulse2~ (or imp2~) is a two-sided impulse oscillator that generates audio signals.
  • gaussian~ The gaussian~ object is a Gaussian oscillator that generates a bell-shaped waveform.
  • glide~ The glide~ object smooths changes in its signal input, creating a portamento effect.
  • xgate2.mc~ The xgate2.mc~ object routes a single input signal to multiple output channels within a multichannel signal.
  • spread~ The spread~ object distributes multiple input audio channels across multiple output channels using equal power panning.
  • mtx~ The mtx~ object acts as an audio signal matrix router, enabling flexible connections between multiple audio inlets and outlets.
  • lace~ The lace~ object interleaves two or more multichannel audio signals.
  • play~ The play~ object plays audio from an array, allowing playback of any segment, at varying speeds (including backwards), and with interpolation.
  • level.m~ The level.m~ object is a simple gain module designed to adjust the decibel level of audio signals.
  • pol2car~ The pol2car~ object converts audio signals from polar coordinates (amplitude and phase) to Cartesian coordinates (real and imaginary parts).
  • voices~ The voices~ object is a polyphonic voice allocator that generates multichannel signals for controlling pitch and gate.
  • xselect2.mc~ xselect2.mc~ is a multichannel signal selector that crossfades between adjacent input channels.
  • rampnoise~ The rampnoise~ object generates band-limited noise by interpolating between random values, producing "random ramps" within a specified frequency range.
  • synth~ synth~ is a Pure Data object that serves as a wrapper for loading and managing monophonic or polyphonic synthesizer abstractions.
  • randpulse~ randpulse~ is a random pulse train oscillator that generates an audio signal alternating between a random value and zero.
  • glide2~ glide2~ is a signal processing object that smooths incoming signals with distinct ramp-up and ramp-down times, effectively creating a portamento or lag effect.
  • square~ square~ is a versatile square wave oscillator that generates audio signals with adjustable pulse width, initial phase, and phase modulation.
  • merge~ The merge~ object combines multiple audio signals, potentially with varying channel lengths, into a single multichannel output.
  • sum~ The sum~ object takes a multi-channel audio signal and sums all its channels into a single mono output signal.
  • cusp~ cusp~ is a chaotic signal generator that implements the difference equation y[n] = a - b * sqrt(abs(y[n-1])).
  • wrap2~ The wrap2~ object wraps an input signal between a specified low and high value.
  • stepnoise~ The stepnoise~ object generates band-limited noise with random, stepped values between -1 and 1.
  • fm~ The fm~ object is a frequency modulation unit that generates audio by modulating the frequency of one sinusoidal oscillator with another.
  • lag2~ The lag2~ object smooths incoming signals, allowing for independent ramp-up and ramp-down times.
  • f2s~ The f2s~ (or float2sig~) object converts incoming float values or lists of floats into audio signals.
  • dbgain~ The dbgain~ object adjusts the gain of an audio signal to a specified decibel (dB) level.
  • record~ The record~ object captures audio signals and stores them into Pure Data arrays, supporting up to 64 channels.
  • pick~ pick~ selects a specific channel from a multichannel audio connection.
  • tabreader The tabreader object reads values from a Pure Data array (table), supporting various interpolation methods like lin, cos, and spline.
  • pan.mc~ pan.mc~ is a multichannel audio panner designed for circular speaker arrays.
  • delay.m~ delay.m~ is an audio effect object that functions as a wrapper for the filterdelay~ object from the ELSE library.
  • pulse~ pulse~ is a pulse train oscillator that generates a signal alternating between 1 and 0.
  • pm~ The pm~ object is a basic phase modulation unit that uses two sinusoidal oscillators, where one modulates the phase of the other.
  • selector The selector object routes incoming data from one of its multiple inlets to its single output.
  • level~ The level~ object is a convenient tool for adjusting the gain of audio signals in decibels (dB).
  • ratio2cents~ The ratio2cents~ object converts a signal representing a rational interval (e.g., a frequency ratio) into an interval expressed in cents.
  • gbman~ gbman~ is a chaotic signal generator based on the Gingerbread Man map.
  • gatehold~ The gatehold~ object holds an incoming gate signal for a specified duration in milliseconds after the gate closes.
  • gaterelease~ The gaterelease~ object releases an audio-rate gate signal after a specified time in milliseconds, allowing for timed gate control even when other gates are held.
  • sig2float~ The sig2float~ object converts audio signals into floating-point numbers.
  • dust2~ The dust2~ object generates random impulses (values between -1 and 1) at random times, controlled by a density parameter.
  • select~ The select~ object selects one of its input audio signals based on a control input, without applying any crossfade.
  • repeat~ The repeat~ object takes an input signal and duplicates it a specified number of times ('n' times).
  • mtx.mc~ The mtx.mc~ object is a multichannel signal routing matrix for Pure Data, designed to route audio signals from multiple input channels to multiple output channels.
  • gain~ The gain~ object provides mono and multichannel gain control for audio signals.
  • tri~ tri~ is a triangular wave oscillator that generates audio signals.
  • bl.square~ bl.square~ is a bandlimited square wave oscillator designed for synthesis, providing anti-aliased output.
  • batch.rec~ batch.rec~ is a convenient Pure Data abstraction for batch recording audio signals to a sound file.
  • resonbank2~ resonbank2~ is a bank of resonant filters designed for sound synthesis.
  • bpbank~ bpbank~ creates a bank of bandpass filters, allowing independent control over the frequency, amplitude, and Q (resonance) of each filter via list inputs.
  • peek~ The peek~ object provides direct, non-interpolated access to Pure Data arrays (tables) for both reading and writing values via messages.
  • gain2~ The gain2~ object is a stereo gain abstraction for Pure Data, designed to adjust the gain of stereo audio signals.
  • cents2ratio~ The cents2ratio~ object converts an audio signal representing an interval in cents into a decimal ratio signal.
  • send2~ The send2~ object transmits audio signals to a corresponding r~ object, similar to send~.
  • rotate.mc~ The rotate.mc~ object performs equal power rotation for multichannel audio signals.
  • decay~ decay~ is a one-pole filter that generates an exponential decay from input impulses.
  • rand.i~ The rand.i~ object generates random integer values within a specified range, triggered by signal transitions.
  • fbsine~ fbsine~ is a sinusoidal audio oscillator that features phase modulation feedback.
  • pvoc.player~ The pvoc.player~ object is an audio file player that offers independent time stretching and pitch shifting capabilities through a phase vocoder.
  • saw~ The saw~ object is a versatile sawtooth wave oscillator that supports negative frequencies, phase synchronization, and phase modulation.
  • saw2~ The saw2~ object is a versatile sawtooth wave oscillator that supports negative frequencies and multichannel operation.
  • limiter~ The limiter~ object in Pure Data functions as an audio dynamics processor, preventing signals from exceeding a specified output level to avoid clipping.
  • sine~ sine~ is a sinusoidal oscillator that generates an audio signal.
  • vco.m~ The vco.m~ object is a polyphonic voltage-controlled oscillator for Pure Data, offering various waveforms including sine, triangle, saw, variable saw, and square.
  • meter4~ The else/meter4~ object functions as a quadraphonic VU-meter, providing RMS/peak amplitude values in dBFS for four incoming audio signals.
  • velvet~ velvet~ is a velvet noise generator that produces audio by randomly choosing positive (1) or negative (-1) impulses at random positions within a specified frequency period.
  • samps2ms~ The samps2ms~ object converts a time value given in samples to milliseconds.
  • rescale~ The rescale~ object transforms incoming audio signals from a specified input range to a desired output range.
  • xselect.mc~ The xselect.mc~ object selects a specific channel from a multichannel audio connection, supporting up to 512 channels.
  • xselect2~ The xselect2~ object selects and crossfades between multiple audio input signals.
  • mix4~ The mix4~ object is a 4-channel audio mixer abstraction that provides independent gain and panning control for each channel.
  • vca.m~ The vca.m~ object is a mono Voltage Controlled Amplifier (VCA) that takes an audio signal and a control voltage (CV) to dynamically adjust the signal's amplitude.
  • pimp~ The pimp~ object is an impulse oscillator that combines the functionality of phasor~ and else/imp~.
  • bitnormal~ The bitnormal~ object filters incoming audio signals, replacing any NaN (Not a Number), positive/negative infinity, or denormal values with zero.
  • ms2samps~ The ms2samps~ object converts time values from milliseconds to the corresponding number of audio samples, based on the current samplerate.
  • lin2db~ The lin2db~ object converts linear amplitude values to decibels full scale (dBFS).
  • bl.osc~ bl.osc~ is a bandlimited oscillator that generates various waveforms like saw, triangle, square, and impulse.
  • xfade.mc~ The xfade.mc~ object crossfades between two multichannel audio sources.
  • pmpd3d~ The pmpd3d~ object is a 3D particle-based physical modeling engine designed for audio synthesis.
  • out.mc~ The out.mc~ object routes multichannel audio signals to specified dac~ output channels, with a default starting channel of 1.
  • autofade~ The autofade~ object provides automatic fade-in and fade-out functionality for multiple audio inputs.
  • multiline~ The multiline~ object performs line-interpolated multiplication of multiple input signals.
  • slice~ The slice~ object splits a multichannel signal into two outputs based on a specified split point.
  • randpulse2~ randpulse2~ is a random pulse train oscillator that generates signals at random intervals, with the average frequency controlled by a "density" parameter.
  • adsr.m~ The adsr.m~ object is a polyphonic ADSR envelope generator.
  • op~ The op~ object performs various signal-rate operations, including comparative (e.g., ==, >), logical (&&, ||, !), bitwise (&, |, ~, ^, <<, >>), and modulus (%) calculations.
  • get~ get~ extracts one or more specific channels from a multichannel audio connection.

docs.title: balance~
docs.text:
The balance~ object performs equal-power (sin/cos) stereo balancing. It takes two signal inputs (left and right channels) and a balance value (from -1 for full left to 1 for full right) as a float or signal. The object outputs the balanced stereo signals and supports multichannel output with the -mc flag.

docs.title: bitnormal~
docs.text:
The bitnormal~ object filters incoming audio signals, replacing any NaN (Not a Number), positive/negative infinity, or denormal values with zero. This ensures that only 'normal' floating-point numbers pass through, making it useful for maintaining signal integrity, especially when working with bitwise operators or expr~ where such values might occur. It also supports multichannel signals.

docs.title: cusp~
docs.text:
cusp~ is a chaotic signal generator that implements the difference equation y[n] = a - b * sqrt(abs(y[n-1])). It produces a chaotic audio signal based on an input frequency and configurable parameters a and b. This object supports multichannel operation and is useful for generating complex, non-linear audio signals.

docs.title: delace~
docs.text:
The delace~ object deinterleaves a multichannel audio signal, splitting it into multiple separate signal outlets. The number of outlets is determined by its creation argument. It can optionally pad deinterleaved signals with zeros using the -z flag to ensure consistent channel counts across outputs.

docs.title: f2s~
docs.text:
The f2s~ (or float2sig~) object converts incoming float values or lists of floats into audio signals. It uses a ramp time, similar to vline~, to smoothly transition between values, preventing clicks or abrupt changes. This object supports multichannel connections, making it suitable for converting control data into audio-rate signals across multiple channels.

docs.text:
The fold~ object performs wavefolding, a waveshaping technique that mirrors audio signals back into a specified low and high range. It is designed for signal-rate processing and supports multichannel audio, with configurable arguments to define its folding range.

docs.text:
The gatedelay~ object delays the "on" state of an incoming gate signal by a specified time, while immediately passing through the "off" state. It supports multichannel operation, allowing individual delay times for each channel when a multichannel signal is provided to its right inlet.

docs.title: gatehold~
docs.text:
The gatehold~ object holds an incoming gate signal for a specified duration in milliseconds after the gate closes. If a new gate begins before the hold time expires, the object re-triggers, extending the hold. It supports multichannel signals and is useful for extending the sustain phase of envelopes or other gate-controlled processes.

docs.title: gaterelease~
docs.text:
The gaterelease~ object releases an audio-rate gate signal after a specified time in milliseconds, allowing for timed gate control even when other gates are held. If the input gate closes before the release time, the object resets, and a release time of 0 or less bypasses the object. It supports multichannel operation.

docs.title: get~
docs.text:
get~ extracts one or more specific channels from a multichannel audio connection. Channels are indexed starting from 1, and you can provide a list of channel numbers to arbitrarily select or remap the output signal. This object is essential for managing and routing individual audio streams within a multichannel signal flow.

docs.title: glide~
docs.text:
The glide~ object smooths changes in its signal input, creating a portamento effect. It takes a glide time in milliseconds as an argument or via its right inlet, and supports both linear and exponential gliding. This object is useful for smoothing parameter changes or creating pitch glides with multichannel signal support.

docs.title: index~
docs.text:
The index~ object reads values from an array without interpolation, using a signal as the index to read the array. It supports multi-channel arrays (up to 64 channels), allowing a specific channel to be selected via an argument or a float input. This object is primarily designed for direct, non-interpolated access to array data, rather than general sample playback.

docs.title: lace~
docs.text:
The lace~ object interleaves two or more multichannel audio signals. It takes multiple multichannel signals as input and outputs a single multichannel signal where the channels from the inputs are interleaved. An optional -z flag can be used to pad shorter multichannel signals with zeros to match the channel count of the longest input.

docs.text:
The lag2~ object smooths incoming signals, allowing for independent ramp-up and ramp-down times. It's similar to lag~ but offers separate control over how quickly a signal rises to a target value versus how quickly it falls. This is useful for creating portamento effects or smoothing control signals.

docs.title: lag~
docs.text:
The lag~ object is a one-pole audio filter that applies an exponential glide or portamento to changes in its signal input. It smooths signal transitions, with a configurable lag time in milliseconds determining the convergence speed. Notably, its rising and descending ramps behave differently, and it supports multichannel audio processing.

docs.text:

docs.title: scope3d~
docs.text:
The scope3d~ object is an experimental 3D oscilloscope display for Pure Data. It visualizes audio signals in three dimensions (X, Y, Z axes) and offers various parameters for controlling its appearance, such as dimensions, zoom, perspective, line width, and refresh rate. Users can interact with the display via mouse dragging for rotation, and it supports multichannel signals where each channel corresponds to a dimension.

docs.title: sig2float~
docs.text:
The sig2float~ object converts audio signals into floating-point numbers. It performs this conversion at a user-defined rate, which can be set via its arguments or right inlet, and supports multichannel signals, outputting a list of floats for each channel. It also provides control over conversion timing, including syncing and sample offset.

docs.title: sigs~
docs.text:
The sigs~ object generates constant multichannel audio signals from a list of numerical arguments. It functions similarly to sig~ but extends its capability to multiple channels, creating a dedicated inlet for each argument. This allows users to easily create and control static multichannel audio signals within a patch.

docs.title: slew2~
docs.text:
The slew2~ object provides independent slew limiting for upward and downward signal ramps. It smoothly transitions an incoming signal to a new target value at a specified amplitude per second, preventing abrupt changes. This is particularly useful for audio or control signals where sudden jumps can cause undesirable artifacts, and it supports multichannel operation.

docs.title: slew~
docs.text:
The slew~ object limits the rate of change of an incoming signal, smoothing transitions between values. It generates a linear ramp towards new target values at a specified amplitude limit per second, rather than over a fixed duration. It supports multichannel signals, applying a single limit to all channels or individual limits per channel.

docs.text:

docs.title: smooth~
docs.text:
The smooth~ object provides a signal smoothing function, transitioning input signals with linear or exponential interpolation over a specified time in milliseconds. This creates a portamento-like effect. It fully supports multichannel signals, allowing independent glide times per channel, and can be reset to the current input value.

docs.title: spread.mc~
docs.text:
The spread.mc~ object distributes multichannel audio input across a specified number of output channels using equal power panning. It allows control over the spread amount, determining how widely input signals are distributed across the output channels. This object is useful for spatializing multichannel audio or creating wider stereo/surround images from narrower inputs.

docs.title: vocoder~
docs.text:
The vocoder~ object is a classic channel vocoder used for cross-synthesis. It takes two audio signals—a synthesis source (carrier) and a control source (modulator)—to produce an output where the spectral characteristics of the modulator are applied to the carrier. Users can control the number of channels, filter Q, and the MIDI frequencies for each channel.

docs.text:

docs.title: xfade~
docs.text:
The xfade~ object crossfades between two audio sources, each supporting 1 to 64 channels. It performs an equal power crossfade by default, but can be set to linear using the -lin flag or lin message. The rightmost inlet controls the mix value from -1 (source A) to 1 (source B).

docs.title: MultiThreading
docs.text:
• abs.pd~ The abs.pd~ object loads a Pure Data patch as a subprocess, making it easier to manage complex patches.
  • pix_image The pix_image object loads image files (like TIFFs and JPEGs) into Pure Data, primarily for use as textures or for bitblitting operations within the GEM environment.
  • coll The coll object in Pure Data is a versatile data collection and manipulation tool.

docs.title: Networking
docs.text:
• pdlink The pdlink object enables simplified communication between different Pure Data instances, including [pd~] subprocesses, over a local network.
  • sfload The sfload object loads sound files into Pure Data arrays, supporting various audio formats like MP3, WAV, and FLAC.
  • pdlink~ pdlink~ enables sending and receiving audio streams between different Pure Data instances, including those on other platforms or forks, and pd~ subprocesses.
  • osc.receive The osc.receive object in Pure Data is designed to receive Open Sound Control (OSC) messages over a network connection.
  • play.file~ play.file~ is a Pure Data object that reads and plays audio files from your computer, supporting various formats like AAC, FLAC, MP3, and WAV.
  • osc.send The osc.send object facilitates sending Open Sound Control (OSC) messages over a network.

docs.title: osc.receive
docs.text:
The osc.receive object in Pure Data is designed to receive Open Sound Control (OSC) messages over a network connection. It acts as an abstraction built upon osc.parse and netreceive, allowing users to easily establish a connection to a specified port and process incoming OSC data.

docs.title: osc.send
docs.text:
The osc.send object facilitates sending Open Sound Control (OSC) messages over a network. It acts as an abstraction built upon osc.format and netsend, allowing users to establish connections to a specified address and port using a connect message. It also provides a disconnect message to close the network connection and outputs its connection status.

This object supports up to 16 audio sends to a single receiver.

docs.title: Neural Networks
docs.text: • neuralnet neuralnet is an artificial neural network Pd external, written in pure C, without any dependencies.

docs.title: neuralnet
docs.text:
neuralnet is an artificial neural network Pd external, written in pure C, without any dependencies. It is inspired by the book "Neural Networks from Scratch in Python" by Harrison Kinsley & Daniel Kukieła. It is an attempt to translate the Python code to C with the Pure Data API, to run neural networks within Pd.

neuralnet creates densely connected neural networks for classification, regression, and binary logistic regression. There are different activation functions and optimizers you can set, and various other settable parameters. The object's help patch and the examples found in the examples directory should cover all the necessary information.

  • Download via Deken.
  • Library developed mainly by Alexandros Drymonitis.
  • Report Bugs/Errors here!
  • Available for .

docs.title: osc.format
docs.text:
The osc.format object converts Pure Data messages into Open Sound Control (OSC) format. It takes anything messages or lists where the first element is an OSC address (starting with /) and formats subsequent elements as float or string arguments. This object is a simplified formatter, primarily used internally by else/osc.send, and does not support OSC bundles.

docs.title: osc.parse
docs.text:
The osc.parse object in Pure Data is used to parse incoming Open Sound Control (OSC) messages. It extracts the address pattern and arguments from an OSC message, providing output in a format more directly usable for OSC message handling compared to the vanilla oscparse object. This object is typically employed for low-level or specialized OSC data parsing, often as part of the osc.receive abstraction.

docs.title: Oscillators
docs.text:
• xmod2~ The xmod2~ object is a signal generator that performs frequency modulation (FM) using two internal oscillators.
  • impulse The impulse object is a control-rate oscillator that generates bangs at a specified frequency, similar to impulse~ but operating at a millisecond resolution.
  • wt2d~ The wt2d~ object is a 2-dimensional wavetable oscillator that reads from a Pure Data array.
  • white~ The white~ object generates white noise using a pseudo-random number generator.
  • cosine~ cosine~ is a versatile cosine oscillator that generates audio signals.
  • bl.tri~ The bl.tri~ object is a bandlimited (anti-aliased) triangle wave oscillator designed for audio synthesis.
  • bl.imp2~ bl.imp2~ is a bandlimited, two-sided impulse oscillator that generates an audio signal.
  • henon~ The henon~ object is a chaotic signal generator that implements the Henon map difference equation.
  • impulse~ The impulse~ object is an impulse oscillator capable of generating signals with negative frequencies.
  • standard~ standard~ is a chaotic signal generator based on the standard map equations.
  • tabreader~ The tabreader~ object reads signal data from a Pure Data array (table).
  • vsaw~ vsaw~ is a versatile audio oscillator that generates variable sawtooth and triangle waveforms.
  • oscnoise~ oscnoise~ is an oscillator that generates a white noise waveform, which can be updated by a bang message.
  • noisi~ The noisi~ object generates bandlimited noise by drawing random numbers at a specified rate in Hz and interpolating between them.
  • damp.osc~ The damp.osc~ object is a damped harmonic motion oscillator that generates audio signals.
  • pm4~ The pm4~ object is a 4-operator phase modulation (FM) synthesizer.
  • pimp The pimp object is a control-rate signal generator, analogous to phasor~ but operating at a control rate with a maximum resolution of 1 ms.
  • parabolic~ parabolic~ is a parabolic oscillator that generates a non-band-limited waveform, similar to a sinusoid but with aliasing.
  • wavetable~ wavetable~ is an interpolating wavetable oscillator that reads audio data from a Pure Data array.
  • bl.wavetable~ bl.wavetable~ is a bandlimited wavetable oscillator that generates audio signals from a specified waveform array.
  • oscbank~ The oscbank~ object generates complex audio signals by combining multiple sine~ oscillators, allowing users to define a list of partials, amplitudes, and phases for each.
  • logistic~ logistic~ is a chaotic signal generator based on the logistic map difference equation.
  • trapezoid~ The trapezoid~ object generates a trapezoidal waveform, functioning as a wavetable oscillator when fed a phase signal (0-1) from phasor~.
  • bl.imp~ bl.imp~ is a bandlimited impulse oscillator that generates an impulse signal at a specified frequency.
  • bl.saw2~ The bl.saw2~ object is a bandlimited, anti-aliased sawtooth oscillator designed for high-quality audio synthesis.
  • pm2~ The pm2~ object is a 2-operator phase modulation (PM) synthesizer.
  • pm6~ The pm6~ object is a 6-operator phase modulation (FM) synthesizer.
  • bl.saw~ The bl.saw~ object generates a bandlimited, anti-aliased sawtooth waveform, suitable for high-quality audio synthesis.
  • sig.m~ The sig.m~ object generates a constant audio signal.
  • function~ The function~ object generates arbitrary waveforms or functions from a list of points and periods.
  • pimpmul~ pimpmul~ is a signal object designed to multiply the phase signal of pimp~ or phasor~ objects.
  • sin~ The sin~ object computes the sine of its signal input, scaled by two pi, outputting the result as a signal.
  • brown~ brown~ is a brown noise generator, also known as Brownian or red noise, which produces a signal whose spectral energy drops 6dB per octave.
  • enrich~ The enrich~ object performs additive synthesis by generating an oscillator bank.
  • impulse2~ impulse2~ (or imp2~) is a two-sided impulse oscillator that generates audio signals.
  • gaussian~ The gaussian~ object is a Gaussian oscillator that generates a bell-shaped waveform.
  • plaits~ The plaits~ object is a versatile audio synthesizer based on the Mutable Instruments Plaits module, offering 24 distinct synthesis engines.
  • xmod~ xmod~ performs cross-modulation of two sine oscillators.
  • lfo.m~ lfo.m~ is a low-frequency oscillator module that generates various waveforms including sine, triangle, saw, vsaw, and square.
  • lfo The lfo object is a control-rate Low Frequency Oscillator that generates various waveforms including sine, triangle, sawtooth, and variable square/sawtooth.
  • cycle~ The cycle~ object is a linear interpolating oscillator that generates an audio signal by repeatedly reading through a waveform.
  • gendyn~ The gendyn~ object generates audio using Dynamic Stochastic Synthesis, based on Xenakis' 'GenDyn' algorithm.
  • randpulse~ randpulse~ is a random pulse train oscillator that generates an audio signal alternating between a random value and zero.
  • pulse The pulse object is a control-rate oscillator that generates a pulse wave, acting like a toggle switch.
  • square~ square~ is a versatile square wave oscillator that generates audio signals with adjustable pulse width, initial phase, and phase modulation.
  • randpulse The randpulse object is a random pulse oscillator that alternates between a random value and zero at a control rate.
  • stepnoise~ The stepnoise~ object generates band-limited noise with random, stepped values between -1 and 1.
  • fm~ The fm~ object is a frequency modulation unit that generates audio by modulating the frequency of one sinusoidal oscillator with another.
  • bl.vsaw~ bl.vsaw~ is a bandlimited variable sawtooth waveform oscillator that can also generate triangular waveforms.
  • triangle~ The triangle~ object generates a variable-duty triangle waveform at signal rate.
  • pvtuner~ pvtuner~ is a frequency-quantized oscillator bank resynthesis object that allows users to impose arbitrary tunings.
  • train~ The train~ object generates a periodic pulse signal that alternates between 1 (on) and 0 (off), with configurable period, pulse width, and an onset phase offset.
  • latoocarfian~ The latoocarfian~ object is a chaotic audio signal generator that implements the Latoocarfian attractor's difference equations.
  • tempo~ The tempo~ object functions as a metronome, generating impulses at a specified tempo in BPM, milliseconds, or Hertz.
  • pulse~ pulse~ is a pulse train oscillator that generates a signal alternating between 1 and 0.
  • pm~ The pm~ object is a basic phase modulation unit that uses two sinusoidal oscillators, where one modulates the phase of the other.
  • float2imp~ The float2imp~ object converts floating-point numbers into sample-accurate audio impulses.
  • gendyn.m~ gendyn.m~ is a wrapper for the else/gendyn~ object, designed to generate dynamic waveforms.
  • blip~ blip~ is a band-limited oscillator that generates waveforms by summing cosines using Discrete-Summation Formulae (DSF).
  • randpulse2 randpulse2 is a random pulse train oscillator that generates pulses (0 or 1, or random values between -1 and 1) at random intervals.
  • noish~ The noish~ object generates bandlimited noise by drawing a random number every n samples and interpolating between these values.
  • phasor The phasor object generates a control-rate sawtooth wave, ramping continuously from 0 to 1 at a specified frequency.
  • quad~ The quad~ object generates a chaotic audio signal based on a quadratic difference equation: y[n] = a * y[n-1]^2 + b * y[n-1] + c.
  • tri~ tri~ is a triangular wave oscillator that generates audio signals.
  • bl.square~ bl.square~ is a bandlimited square wave oscillator designed for synthesis, providing anti-aliased output.
  • fbsine~ fbsine~ is a sinusoidal audio oscillator that features phase modulation feedback.
  • saw~ The saw~ object is a versatile sawtooth wave oscillator that supports negative frequencies, phase synchronization, and phase modulation.
  • fbsine2~ The fbsine2~ object is a non-interpolating sound generator that produces a feedback sine chaotic signal.
  • saw2~ The saw2~ object is a versatile sawtooth wave oscillator that supports negative frequencies and multichannel operation.
  • sine~ sine~ is a sinusoidal oscillator that generates an audio signal.
  • vco.m~ The vco.m~ object is a polyphonic voltage-controlled oscillator for Pure Data, offering various waveforms including sine, triangle, saw, variable saw, and square.
  • pm6.m~ pm6.m~ is a six-operator FM (phase modulation) synthesizer, inspired by the DX7, producing stereo output.
  • pvharm~ pvharm~ is an audio harmonizer that transposes an input signal into two pitches.
  • pink~ The pink~ object generates pink noise, an audio signal characterized by constant power per octave, with its energy decreasing by 3dB per octave.
  • pimp~ The pimp~ object is an impulse oscillator that combines the functionality of phasor~ and else/imp~.
  • bl.osc~ bl.osc~ is a bandlimited oscillator that generates various waveforms like saw, triangle, square, and impulse.
  • randpulse2~ randpulse2~ is a random pulse train oscillator that generates signals at random intervals, with the average frequency controlled by a "density" parameter.

docs.title: bl.tri~
docs.text:
The bl.tri~ object is a bandlimited (anti-aliased) triangle wave oscillator designed for audio synthesis. It generates a clean triangle waveform, accepting frequency input in Hertz or MIDI pitch, and supports phase offset/modulation and synchronization (hard or soft sync). It also features multichannel capabilities.

AI Generated

docs.title: bl.vsaw~
docs.text:
bl.vsaw~ is a bandlimited variable sawtooth waveform oscillator that can also generate triangular waveforms. It is anti-aliased for high-quality audio synthesis and supports frequency input in both Hertz and MIDI pitch. The object includes inlets for controlling waveform width, phase synchronization, and phase offset, and it supports multichannel operation.

docs.title: bl.wavetable~
docs.text:
bl.wavetable~ is a bandlimited wavetable oscillator that generates audio signals from a specified waveform array. It employs upsampling and filtering to minimize aliasing, especially during hard sync and phase modulation. It supports various interpolation methods, phase synchronization, and phase modulation, with options for frequency input in Hz or MIDI pitch.

docs.title: brown~
docs.text:
brown~ is a brown noise generator, also known as Brownian or red noise, which produces a signal whose spectral energy drops 6dB per octave. It operates as a bounded random walk, generating new steps based on a pseudo-random number algorithm, and allows for custom step sizes and seed values for reproducible sequences.

docs.title: cosine~
docs.text:
cosine~ is a versatile cosine oscillator that generates audio signals. It supports negative frequencies, multichannel operation, and offers advanced phase control through dedicated inlets for phase synchronization and phase modulation. Users can specify frequency in Hertz or MIDI pitch, and choose between hard or soft synchronization modes.

docs.title: cycle~
docs.text:
The cycle~ object is a linear interpolating oscillator that generates an audio signal by repeatedly reading through a waveform. It can use an internal cosine wave or custom waveforms from Pure Data arrays or buffer~ objects, with controls for frequency, phase, buffer offset, and size. This object is fundamental for creating periodic audio signals and can be used as a building block for various synthesis techniques.

docs.title: damp.osc~
docs.text:
The damp.osc~ object is a damped harmonic motion oscillator that generates audio signals. It accepts frequency (in Hz or MIDI pitch) and a decay time in milliseconds as inputs. The object can be triggered by zero-to-non-zero signal transitions or control-rate lists, providing both cosine and sine phase outputs.

docs.title: fbsine~
docs.text:
fbsine~ is a sinusoidal audio oscillator that features phase modulation feedback. It supports negative frequencies, phase synchronization, and multichannel operation, allowing for complex sound generation. The object includes a built-in mean average filter in its feedback loop, which can be toggled, to smooth out high frequencies and control chaotic behavior.

docs.title: float2imp~
docs.text:
The float2imp~ object converts floating-point numbers into sample-accurate audio impulses. It can generate an impulse immediately upon receiving a float, or output a stored impulse value when triggered by a bang. This object is useful for precisely timing events or triggering audio processes within an audio block.

docs.title: fm~
docs.text:
The fm~ object is a frequency modulation unit that generates audio by modulating the frequency of one sinusoidal oscillator with another. It takes inputs for carrier frequency, modulation index, and modulation frequency (as a ratio of the carrier). This object also supports multichannel audio processing.

docs.title: impulse
docs.text:
The impulse object is a control-rate oscillator that generates bangs at a specified frequency, similar to impulse~ but operating at a millisecond resolution. Unlike tempo or metro, it offers immediate response to rate changes, making it ideal for precise timing and event triggering. It supports frequency and initial phase arguments, and its internal phase can be reset.

docs.text:

This object also features multichannel capabilities and can interpret frequency input in either Hertz or MIDI pitch.

docs.text:
The impulse~ object is an impulse oscillator capable of generating signals with negative frequencies. It features inlets for phase synchronization and phase modulation, allowing for complex rhythmic and timbral control. This versatile object supports multichannel operation and can interpret frequency inputs in either Hertz or MIDI pitch.

docs.title: logistic~
docs.text:
logistic~ is a chaotic signal generator based on the logistic map difference equation. It produces a chaotic audio signal, with its output rate controlled by a frequency in Hertz and a 'p' parameter (0 to 1) influencing the chaotic behavior.

docs.title: parabolic~
docs.text:
parabolic~ is a parabolic oscillator that generates a non-band-limited waveform, similar to a sinusoid but with aliasing. It supports negative frequencies, phase synchronization via control data or impulse signals, and phase modulation. This object also features comprehensive multichannel audio support and can interpret frequency inputs in either Hertz or MIDI pitch.

docs.title: phasor
docs.text:
The phasor object generates a control-rate sawtooth wave, ramping continuously from 0 to 1 at a specified frequency. It functions similarly to the audio-rate phasor~ but is designed for control signals, with a default rate period of 1ms. It can be initialized with a frequency and phase offset, and its phase can be synchronized via an inlet.

docs.title: pimp
docs.text:
The pimp object is a control-rate signal generator, analogous to phasor~ but operating at a control rate with a maximum resolution of 1 ms. It generates a periodic signal based on an input frequency in Hertz and an initial phase offset. It also provides an inlet for phase synchronization and can output a bang at each period transition.

docs.title: pimp~
docs.text:
The pimp~ object is an impulse oscillator that combines the functionality of phasor~ and else/imp~. It outputs a phase signal and generates an impulse at the start of each phase period, supporting both hard and soft synchronization. It can be controlled by frequency in Hertz or MIDI pitch, and supports multichannel operation for complex signal generation and modulation.

docs.title: pink~
docs.text:
The pink~ object generates pink noise, an audio signal characterized by constant power per octave, with its energy decreasing by 3dB per octave. It supports setting a seed for reproducible noise sequences and allows adjustment of the "pinkness" by specifying the number of octaves.

docs.title: pm2~
docs.text:
The pm2~ object is a 2-operator phase modulation (PM) synthesizer. It allows each of its two sine wave oscillators to modulate itself and the other, offering control over frequency, ratio, detune, panning, and volume for each operator, as well as a modulation matrix. It also supports multichannel audio.

docs.title: pm4~
docs.text:
The pm4~ object is a 4-operator phase modulation (FM) synthesizer. It allows for complex sound generation by enabling each of its sine wave oscillators to modulate itself and any other operator. Users can control parameters such as frequency, ratio, detune, panning, volume, and the modulation matrix for each operator, and it supports multichannel audio.

docs.title: pm6.m~
docs.text:
pm6.m~ is a six-operator FM (phase modulation) synthesizer, inspired by the DX7, producing stereo output. It takes MIDI pitch input and allows control over each operator's level, ratio, and panning, as well as a modulation index matrix for feedback and feedforward modulation. It also features a master level control and pitch transposition.

docs.text:

docs.text:

docs.title: pvtuner~
docs.text:
pvtuner~ is a frequency-quantized oscillator bank resynthesis object that allows users to impose arbitrary tunings. It supports custom scales defined by lists of frequencies, can interpolate between different scales, and features transposition and a synthesis threshold. It can also set a base frequency using a MIDI note.

docs.text:
The randpulse object is a random pulse oscillator that alternates between a random value and zero at a control rate. It accepts negative frequencies and has inlets for pulse width and phase synchronization. This object functions as an oscillator with a running phase, operating at a user-defined rate.

It features dedicated inlets for phase synchronization (including hard and soft sync modes) and phase modulation, allowing for complex timbral manipulation. Frequency input can be specified in Hertz or MIDI pitch, and initial phase offsets can be set via arguments or inlets.

It offers flexible frequency input modes (Hertz or MIDI pitch) and can operate in soft sync mode for a less harsh sound. Additionally, saw~ provides robust multichannel support, allowing for complex polyphonic or layered synthesis.

docs.text:

docs.title: tempo~
docs.text:
The tempo~ object functions as a metronome, generating impulses at a specified tempo in BPM, milliseconds, or Hertz. It can be turned on/off via a gate input and synchronized with a bang or impulse. Additionally, it features a 'swing' parameter for introducing controlled random timing deviations, which can be made reproducible using a seed.

docs.title: triangle~
docs.text:
The triangle~ object generates a variable-duty triangle waveform at signal rate. It uses a phase input (0-1) to read its internal wavetable, effectively creating an oscillator when driven by a phasor~. The second inlet controls the peak position, allowing for waveforms ranging from sawtooth to symmetric triangles, and its output range can be adjusted via lo and hi messages.

docs.title: Partial Tracking
docs.text:
• pvoc.freeze~ The pvoc.freeze~ object is a phase vocoder-based audio processor that allows for "freezing" a sound.
  • pvwarpb~ pvwarpb~ is a spectral warper that applies an internal frequency warping function to audio signals.
  • pvoc~ The pvoc~ object performs phase vocoding, a technique for time-stretching or pitch-shifting audio by analyzing its frequency content.
  • resent~ resent~ is a spectral sample buffer that captures an input audio signal for manipulation.
  • reanimator~ The reanimator~ object performs audio texture mapping by analyzing a "texture" sound using spectral analysis (FFT) and then re-synthesizing audio based on a "driver" sound.
  • pileup~ The pileup~ object performs spectral persistence by maintaining amplitude and phase information in frequency bins.
  • framedelta~ The framedelta~ object calculates the running phase deviation of an FFT analysis.
  • pvoc.player~ The pvoc.player~ object is an audio file player that offers independent time stretching and pitch shifting capabilities through a phase vocoder.
  • pvoc.live~ The pvoc.live~ object performs independent time stretching and pitch shifting on live audio input using granulation.
  • pvharm~ pvharm~ is an audio harmonizer that transposes an input signal into two pitches.
  • morph~ The morph~ object performs a spectral crossfade between the amplitudes and phases of two input signals.
  • freeze~ The freeze~ object captures a spectral snapshot of an incoming audio signal and resynthesizes it using a bank of oscillators, effectively "freezing" the sound.
  • residency~ The residency~ object samples audio input into an internal buffer, primarily for spectral processing.

docs.title: freeze~
docs.text:
The freeze~ object captures a spectral snapshot of an incoming audio signal and resynthesizes it using a bank of oscillators, effectively "freezing" the sound. Based on sigmund~ for analysis and resynthesis, it allows for the sustained playback of a specific moment from the input audio, controlled by a non-zero input to freeze/refreeze or a zero input to unfreeze.

docs.text:

It can resynthesize audio using an oscillator bank, allowing for control over low/high frequencies, transposition, and a synthesis threshold. It also features options for spectral "feedback" and various persistence modes (amplitude/phase, amplitude only, inverse).

docs.title: Patching
docs.text:
• universal The universal object sends messages to all instances of a specified object type within the current Pure Data patch.
  • properties The properties object in Pure Data sends a bang when its parent patch's properties are accessed, typically via a right-click menu.
  • canvas.setname canvas.setname assigns a symbolic name to a Pure Data canvas, enabling it to receive messages.
  • funnel The funnel object receives data from multiple inlets and outputs it through a single outlet, tagging the incoming data with its inlet number.
  • canvas.zoom The canvas.zoom object reports the zoom status of a Pure Data patch window.
  • append The cyclone/append object concatenates a predefined message to the end of any incoming message.
  • retrieve The retrieve object in Pure Data fetches data from named receive objects (r, else/receiver) or GUI elements that have built-in receive names.
  • route2 The route2 object functions similarly to route, but it preserves the list selector in its output messages and supports both float and symbol arguments for routing.
  • buddy The buddy object synchronizes incoming messages from its inlets.
  • canvas.gop The canvas.gop object retrieves information about a "graph on parent" (GOP), which is a subpatch displayed graphically within its parent patch.
  • canvas.vis The canvas.vis object reports the visibility status of a Pure Data window.
  • a2l The a2l object converts any incoming message into a list, providing a unified data format for further processing.
  • canvas.active The canvas.active object reports the activity status of a Pure Data canvas, outputting 1 when active (front-most window) and 0 when inactive.
  • anal The anal object reports the number of times it receives a specific number pair, effectively building a histogram of occurrences.
  • synth~ synth~ is a Pure Data object that serves as a wrapper for loading and managing monophonic or polyphonic synthesizer abstractions.
  • canvas.name The canvas.name object outputs the symbolic name of the Pure Data canvas it resides in.
  • args The args object in Pure Data manages and manipulates arguments passed to an abstraction.
  • loadbanger The loadbanger (or lb) object in Pure Data sends "bang" messages when a patch is loaded, when it receives any message, or when clicked.
  • canvas.edit The canvas.edit object reports the edit status of a Pure Data canvas, outputting 1 when in edit mode and 0 in run mode.
  • message The message object in Pure Data serves as a versatile container for storing and outputting any type of message, functioning similarly to a message box but as an object.
  • change~ The change~ object detects if an incoming audio signal is changing and its direction.
  • dollsym The dollsym object in Pure Data expands dollar-prefixed symbols like $0-x or $1-y into their corresponding values within a patch.
  • loadmess The loadmess object sends a predefined message automatically when its containing patch is loaded, or when it receives a bang message.
  • click The click object sends a bang message when its parent patch (abstraction or subpatch) is clicked.
  • gemargs The gemargs object retrieves arguments and initialization messages passed to its parent patch, enabling consistent object initialization patterns within GEM abstractions.
  • active The active object reports the activity status of a Pure Data patch window.
  • pv The pv object in Pure Data functions as a private variable, storing any type of message.

docs.title: a2l
docs.text:
The a2l object converts any incoming message into a list, providing a unified data format for further processing. This utility is primarily used for control flow within Pure Data patches, allowing various message types to be handled consistently as lists. It is part of the zexy library and is marked as deprecated.

docs.title: buddy
docs.text:
The buddy object synchronizes incoming messages from its inlets. It waits until all inlets have received a message, then outputs all stored messages in right-to-left order. This is useful for ensuring that a set of messages, potentially arriving asynchronously, are processed together in a specific sequence.

docs.title: canvas.active
docs.text:
The canvas.active object reports the activity status of a Pure Data canvas, outputting 1 when active (front-most window) and 0 when inactive. It can query activity for parent patches using a depth argument, or, with the -name flag, output the active canvas's name, enabling dynamic patching and communication with the active window.

docs.title: canvas.gop
docs.text:
The canvas.gop object retrieves information about a "graph on parent" (GOP), which is a subpatch displayed graphically within its parent patch. It outputs details such as the GOP's status, size, and coordinates. Users can query this information for the current patch or for parent patches by specifying a depth argument.

docs.title: canvas.name
docs.text:
The canvas.name object outputs the symbolic name of the Pure Data canvas it resides in. It can also retrieve the names of parent canvases by specifying a depth level, or the main patch window's name using the -env flag. This functionality is primarily used for dynamic patching and establishing communication between different parts of a Pure Data patch hierarchy.

docs.title: canvas.setname
docs.text:
canvas.setname assigns a symbolic name to a Pure Data canvas, enabling it to receive messages. Similar to namecanvas, it uniquely allows naming parent patches using an optional depth argument. It also supports an 'abstraction mode' to name the main window of a parent patch, ensuring the abstraction owner is always targeted.

docs.title: change~
docs.text:
The change~ object detects if an incoming audio signal is changing and its direction. It outputs 1 if the signal is increasing, -1 if it's decreasing, and 0 if it remains constant. This object is useful for generating control signals based on the dynamic behavior of an audio stream.

docs.title: click
docs.text:
The click object sends a bang message when its parent patch (abstraction or subpatch) is clicked. It is primarily used to enable non-graphical abstractions to respond to user clicks, similar to how compiled objects behave. It has an argument to switch between 'abstraction' mode (default, responds to clicks on the abstraction's parent) and 'subpatch' mode (responds to clicks on the subpatch's owner window).

docs.title: dollsym
docs.text:
The dollsym object in Pure Data expands dollar-prefixed symbols like $0-x or $1-y into their corresponding values within a patch. It is particularly useful for creating flexible abstractions, as it can resolve these symbols based on the current patch or a specified parent patch's context using a 'depth' argument. Symbols without dollar arguments are passed through unchanged.

docs.title: gemargs
docs.text:
The gemargs object retrieves arguments and initialization messages passed to its parent patch, enabling consistent object initialization patterns within GEM abstractions. It distinguishes between regular arguments, output as lists, and initialization messages, which retain their selector. This allows for flexible parameter passing and setup in complex Pure Data patches.

docs.title: message
docs.text:
The message object in Pure Data serves as a versatile container for storing and outputting any type of message, functioning similarly to a message box but as an object. It facilitates flexible data flow by allowing messages to be split by commas and enabling direct sending to named receives via semicolons. This object is fundamental for managing and routing data within a Pd patch.

docs.title: properties
docs.text:
The properties object in Pure Data sends a bang when its parent patch's properties are accessed, typically via a right-click menu. This allows for dynamic configuration or display of properties for graphical subpatches or abstractions. It supports two modes: "abstraction" (default) and "subpatch", controlled by its creation argument.

docs.title: Physical Modeling
docs.text:
• tLink2D The tLink2D object calculates properties of a link connecting two masses in a 2D physical simulation.
  • mass The mass object simulates a physical mass, calculating its position, velocity, and applied force based on incoming forces and its defined weight.
  • pluck~ The pluck~ object implements the Karplus-Strong algorithm for physical modeling synthesis, simulating plucked string sounds.
  • pmpd2d The pmpd2d object implements a 2D mass-spring-damper physical model, designed for particle-based simulations in Pure Data.
  • link3D The link3D object simulates a physical link between two masses in a 3D environment.
  • pmpd~ The pmpd~ object is a 1D particle-based physical modeling external for audio synthesis in Pure Data.
  • mass3D The mass3D object simulates a mass in a 3D space, calculating its position and velocity based on applied forces, weight, damping, and boundary conditions.
  • mass2D The mass2D object simulates a 2D mass, calculating its position and velocity based on applied forces, weight, and damping.
  • tLink3D The tLink3D object in Pure Data is designed for 3D physical modeling, simulating a link between two masses.
  • iCircle2D The iCircle2D object simulates circular interactions in 2D space, applying various forces and displacements to an interacting mass.
  • iCircle3D The iCircle3D object simulates the interaction of a named mass with a 3D circular plane.
  • pmpd3d The pmpd3d object implements 3D physical models based on a mass-spring-damper system.
  • link2D The link2D object simulates a physical link between two masses in a 2D space.
  • tPlane3D The tPlane3D object simulates interactions involving a 3D plane.
  • newWave The newWave object renders a dynamic, waving square using a mass-spring system, acting as a geometric object within GEM.
  • link The link object calculates forces between two masses, simulating a physical connection with configurable rigidity and damping.
  • plaits~ The plaits~ object is a versatile audio synthesizer based on the Mutable Instruments Plaits module, offering 24 distinct synthesis engines.
  • resonator2~ The resonator2~ object is a resonant complex one-pole filter, designed to be excited by impulses.
  • pluck.m~ The pluck.m~ object is a polyphonic physical modeling synthesizer for plucked string sounds, acting as a wrapper around the pluck~ object from the ELSE library.
  • iLine2D The iLine2D object simulates the interaction of a named "mass" with a defined 2D line.
  • pmpd2d~ The pmpd2d~ object performs 2D particle-based physical modeling for audio synthesis.
  • iSeg2D The iSeg2D object simulates the physical interaction of a mass with a 2D segment.
  • iPlane3D The iPlane3D object simulates the physical interaction between a 3D plane and a specified mass, such as a circle.
  • pmpd The pmpd object in Pure Data implements a mass-spring-damper physical model.
  • iCylinder3D The iCylinder3D object simulates the interaction between a 'mass' and a 3D cylinder.
  • pmpd3d~ The pmpd3d~ object is a 3D particle-based physical modeling engine designed for audio synthesis.
  • iAmbient3D The iAmbient3D object simulates ambient interactions within a 3D space.

docs.title: iAmbient3D
docs.text:
The iAmbient3D object simulates ambient interactions within a 3D space. It applies various forces, including constant, random, and damping, to interacting "masses" and defines spatial boundaries for their movement. This object is designed to model environmental influences on objects in a 3D simulation.

docs.title: iCircle2D
docs.text:
The iCircle2D object simulates circular interactions in 2D space, applying various forces and displacements to an interacting mass. It defines a circular region with adjustable center and radii, influencing the mass with parameters like constant normal/tangential forces, rigidity, damping, and gravitational effects. This object is designed for physical modeling and interaction simulations.

docs.title: tLink2D
docs.text:
The tLink2D object calculates properties of a link connecting two masses in a 2D physical simulation. It takes the 2D positions of two masses as input and outputs their distance, the link's elongation speed, its orientation, and the position of its center. This object is designed to compute and output forces, making it suitable for building interactive physical models.

docs.title: tLink3D
docs.text:
The tLink3D object in Pure Data is designed for 3D physical modeling, simulating a link between two masses. It computes and outputs forces based on their interaction, providing data such as distance, elongation speed, center position, and orientation of the link. Additionally, it can visualize the 3D link within the Gem graphical environment.

docs.title: tPlane3D
docs.text:

docs.title: Procedural
docs.text:
• l.readsvg l.readsvg is a pdlua object designed to read SVG files created or edited in Inkscape.
  • chance~ The chance~ object probabilistically routes an incoming bang to one of its outlets based on a set of weighted chances.
  • segregate The segregate object sorts incoming messages by their data type, sending them to dedicated outlets.
  • drunkard The drunkard object generates a sequence of pseudo-random numbers based on a drunkard's walk algorithm.
  • loop The loop object in Pure Data provides a flexible iteration mechanism, functioning as a counter that can increment or decrement through a specified range.
  • rec The rec object records incoming messages across multiple tracks and plays them back.
  • drip The drip object unfolds a package (list) into a sequence of individual elements, outputting them one by one with a configurable delay in milliseconds.
  • l.attrfilter The l.attrfilter object in Pure Data (using pdlua) filters elements from an SVG file loaded with l.readsvg based on attributes such as stroke, fill, or ID.
  • mono~ The mono~ object acts as a monophonic signal voice manager, emulating monophonic synthesizer behavior.
  • niagara The niagara object divides an incoming message or list into two sub-packages.
  • speedlim The speedlim object controls the rate at which messages are passed through.
  • part_targetsize The part_targetsize object is a GEM particle object that dynamically adjusts the size of particles over time.
  • count The count object in Pure Data generates a sequence of numbers, incrementing or decrementing between a defined minimum and maximum value.
  • nmess The nmess object acts as a message gate, allowing a specified number of messages (n) to pass through its left outlet.
  • euclid The euclid object implements the Euclidean rhythm algorithm, which distributes a given number of "hits" as evenly as possible over a specified number of "steps".
  • abs.pd~ The abs.pd~ object loads a Pure Data patch as a subprocess, making it easier to manage complex patches.
  • counter The counter object in Pure Data increments or decrements an integer value within a specified range.
  • timed.gate~ The timed.gate~ object generates a timed gate signal or control output.
  • loadbanger The loadbanger (or lb) object in Pure Data sends "bang" messages when a patch is loaded, when it receives any message, or when clicked.
  • repeat The repeat object in Pure Data outputs any incoming message a specified number of times.
  • tempo The tempo object functions as a versatile metronome, sending bangs at a specified rate in BPM, milliseconds, or Hertz.
  • speed The speed object facilitates gradual changes in tempo, enabling accelerando or ritardando effects.
  • loadmess The loadmess object sends a predefined message automatically when its containing patch is loaded, or when it receives a bang message.
  • limit The limit object controls the rate of messages, ensuring a minimum time interval between outputs.
  • mono The else/mono object manages incoming MIDI note messages to enforce monophonic behavior, allowing only one note to sound at a time based on configurable priority modes (last, high, or low note).
  • chrono The chrono object functions as a versatile stopwatch or timer.
  • voices The voices object manages voice allocation for polyphonic synthesizers, offering advanced features beyond poly.
  • uzi The uzi object generates a sequence of bangs and corresponding counter values, useful for iterating through a specified number of steps.
  • clock The clock object generates bangs at a regular tempo, functioning as either a "main" or "synced" clock.
  • sequencer~ The sequencer~ object outputs values from a predefined sequence when triggered by a signal (0 to non-0 transition) or a bang.

docs.title: abs.pd~
docs.text:
The abs.pd~ object loads a Pure Data patch as a subprocess, making it easier to manage complex patches. It automatically handles DSP state for both the parent and sub-process, and facilitates MIDI input to the subprocess. Users can interact with the subprocess by sending messages or opening its patch directly.

docs.title: chrono
docs.text:
The chrono object functions as a versatile stopwatch or timer. It can be started, stopped, and rewound using float and bang messages, and its timer duration can be set with a list of minutes and seconds. It outputs the current time as a symbol and sends a bang when the timer completes.

docs.title: clock
docs.text:
The clock object generates bangs at a regular tempo, functioning as either a "main" or "synced" clock. A main clock sets the tempo in BPM and controls synced clock instances, which operate at a relative tempo (multiplier) to the main clock. It is primarily used for precise timing and synchronization within a Pure Data patch.

docs.title: count
docs.text:
The count object in Pure Data generates a sequence of numbers, incrementing or decrementing between a defined minimum and maximum value. It supports counting up, down, or alternating directions, and can be reset or set to a specific value. It outputs the current count and can send a bang when reaching its limits.

docs.title: counter
docs.text:
The counter object in Pure Data increments or decrements an integer value within a specified range. It can count up, down, or alternate directions, and provides various messages to control its state, limits, and output behavior. It is a fundamental building block often used for creating step sequencers or managing indices.

docs.title: drip
docs.text:
The drip object unfolds a package (list) into a sequence of individual elements, outputting them one by one with a configurable delay in milliseconds. It functions like a medical drip, allowing control over the "drop speed." Scheduling can be enabled or disabled, with n=0 enabling scheduling and negative values disabling it.

docs.title: euclid
docs.text:
The euclid object implements the Euclidean rhythm algorithm, which distributes a given number of "hits" as evenly as possible over a specified number of "steps". It outputs bangs for each hit and rest, and a float (1 for hit, 0 for rest) indicating the rhythm pattern. This object is useful for generating rhythmic sequences.

docs.title: l.readsvg
docs.text:
l.readsvg is a pdlua object designed to read SVG files created or edited in Inkscape. It belongs to the pdlua project pd-upic, which—as the name suggests—is related to the UPIC system developed by Iannis Xenakis.

The object can read and play drawings, provided they follow specific rules explained in this guide.

docs.title: loadbanger
docs.text:
The loadbanger (or lb) object in Pure Data sends "bang" messages when a patch is loaded, when it receives any message, or when clicked. It supports different modes for sending bangs: a default "load" mode, an "init" mode (using the -init flag) which sends bangs earlier for dynamic patching, and a "finalize" mode (using the -fin flag) for actions after patch loading. The number of outputs can be configured by an argument.

docs.title: mono~
docs.text:
The mono~ object acts as a monophonic signal voice manager, emulating monophonic synthesizer behavior. It processes MIDI note messages (note and velocity pairs) and outputs corresponding signal-rate pitch and velocity values, along with impulses for retriggering envelopes. Key features include configurable note priority modes (last, high, low) and a portamento setting for pitch gliding.

docs.title: nmess
docs.text:
The nmess object acts as a message gate, allowing a specified number of messages (n) to pass through its left outlet. Once n messages have passed, subsequent messages are routed to its right outlet. A bang or a float (to set n) in the right inlet resets the gate, allowing messages to pass through the left outlet again.

The object also supports tempo multiplication for subdivisions and allows for seeding its internal random number generator for reproducible swing patterns.

docs.title: uzi
docs.text:
The uzi object generates a sequence of bangs and corresponding counter values, useful for iterating through a specified number of steps. It outputs a bang from its left outlet for each step, a float representing the current count from its right outlet, and a final bang from its middle outlet upon completion. Control messages like pause, resume, and offset allow for flexible loop management, making it ideal for tasks such as array indexing.

docs.title: Reverb
docs.text:
• plate.rev~ The plate.rev~ object is a Pure Data abstraction that implements a stereo plate reverb audio effect.
  • free.rev~ The free.rev~ object implements a stereo reverb effect based on the Freeverb algorithm, utilizing parallel comb filters and series allpass filters.
  • fdn.rev~ fdn.rev~ is a feedback delay network (FDN) reverberator designed to simulate late reflections, commonly known as the reverb tail.
  • mono.rev~ The mono.rev~ object is a reverb abstraction that takes a mono audio input and produces a stereo audio output.
  • stereo.rev~ stereo.rev~ is a stereo input/output reverb abstraction, providing two independent reverb channels.
  • fbdelay~ The fbdelay~ object creates a feedback delay line, useful for delay effects, reverberation, and comb filtering.
  • echo.rev~ echo.rev~ is an echo/reverb abstraction designed to produce early reflections in a reverb algorithm or function as a standalone effect.
  • convolve~ convolve~ is a partitioned impulse response (IR) convolution external for Pure Data.
  • giga.rev~ The giga.rev~ object implements a Gigaverb-based reverberation algorithm, transforming a mono audio input into a stereo output.
  • plate.rev.m~ The plate.rev.m~ object is a Pure Data audio effect that acts as a wrapper for the else/plate.rev~ object, providing a plate reverberation effect.
  • comb.rev~ The comb.rev~ object implements a comb filter, functioning as a versatile audio effect for creating delays, reverberation, and resonant textures.
  • allpass.rev~ The allpass.rev~ object functions as an allpass filter, a delay, and a reverberator.
  • comb.filt~ The comb.filt~ object implements a feedback comb filter, designed primarily for use as a resonator.
  • conv~ The conv~ object performs partitioned convolution, primarily used for applying impulse responses to audio signals.

docs.title: allpass.rev~
docs.text:
The allpass.rev~ object functions as an allpass filter, a delay, and a reverberator. It allows setting a delay time and a reverberation/decay time (t60), which measures the time for an impulse to fall 60dB in energy. Alternatively, it can operate in a "gain mode" where the decay is controlled by a gain coefficient.

docs.title: comb.rev~
docs.text:
The comb.rev~ object implements a comb filter, functioning as a versatile audio effect for creating delays, reverberation, and resonant textures. It allows control over delay time (D), input gain (a), feedforward gain (b), and feedback gain (c) to shape the sound.

docs.title: convolve~
docs.text:
convolve~ is a partitioned impulse response (IR) convolution external for Pure Data. The only creation argument is the partition size, which also determines the amount of delay between the dry and wet signal (i.e., pre-delay). Partition size must be a multiple of Pd's default block size: 64 samples (1.4512ms @ 44100Hz).

docs.title: conv~
docs.text:
The conv~ object performs partitioned convolution, primarily used for applying impulse responses to audio signals. It takes an input audio signal and convolves it with a specified impulse response, which is loaded from a table. Users can set the impulse response table and adjust the partition size for latency and performance trade-offs.

docs.title: echo.rev~
docs.text:
echo.rev~ is an echo/reverb abstraction designed to produce early reflections in a reverb algorithm or function as a standalone effect. It features feedforward lines and allows control over the number of echo stages and room size.

docs.title: fdn.rev~
docs.text:
fdn.rev~ is a feedback delay network (FDN) reverberator designed to simulate late reflections, commonly known as the reverb tail. Its primary parameters are the reverberation decay time (t60) and high-frequency damping, which controls how quickly higher frequencies decay. The object's internal delay lines can be configured using a list of values to set the matrix size and individual delay times, which can be spaced linearly or exponentially.
