docs.title: free.rev~
docs.text:
The free.rev~ object implements a stereo reverb effect based on the Freeverb algorithm, utilizing parallel comb filters and series allpass filters. It allows control over decay time, high-frequency damping, stereo width, and the dry/wet mix, making it suitable for simulating various room acoustics.

docs.title: giga.rev~
docs.text:
The giga.rev~ object implements a Gigaverb-based reverberation algorithm, transforming a mono audio input into a stereo output. It offers extensive control over room characteristics, including size, decay time, high-frequency damping, input bandwidth, and the levels of dry, early, and late reflections, as well as stereo spread. This allows for simulating various acoustic spaces from small rooms to large canyons.

docs.title: mono.rev~
docs.text:
The mono.rev~ object is a reverb abstraction that takes a mono audio input and produces a stereo audio output. It is based on a feedback delay network, similar to Pure Data's rev3~, and includes parameters for decay time, high-frequency damping, room size, and wet/dry mix. The 'room size' parameter can produce unusual artifacts when changed, which can be mitigated by clearing the delay buffers.

docs.title: Routing
docs.text:
• out~ out~ is a convenient Pure Data abstraction for managing audio output.
  • unmerge~ unmerge~ separates the channels of a multichannel signal into groups of a specified size.
  • grab The grab object in Pure Data sends a message to another object and captures its output, routing it through grab's own outlets.
  • gemreceive gemreceive is an ordered message receiver for the GEM (Graphics Environment for Multimedia) environment.
  • chance~ The chance~ object probabilistically routes an incoming bang to one of its outlets based on a set of weighted chances.
  • xgate~ The xgate~ object routes an incoming audio signal to one of its multiple outlets, supporting up to 500 channels.
  • pdlink The pdlink object enables simplified communication between different Pure Data instances, including [pd~] subprocesses, over a local network.
  • gate~ The gate~ object routes an incoming audio signal from its second inlet to one of its multiple outlets.
  • out8~ The out8~ object is an octaphonic (8-channel) audio output abstraction for Pure Data.
  • segregate The segregate object sorts incoming messages by their data type, sending them to dedicated outlets.
  • xgate2~ xgate2~ routes a single input signal to a specified number of output channels, applying crossfading between adjacent channels.
  • universal The universal object sends messages to all instances of a specified object type within the current Pure Data patch.
  • gate The gate object functions as a message router, directing incoming messages from its second inlet to one of its multiple outlets.
  • bondo The bondo object in Pure Data synchronizes and routes incoming messages.
  • funnel The funnel object receives data from multiple inlets and outputs it through a single outlet, tagging the incoming data with its inlet number.
  • forward The forward object sends messages to various destinations, allowing the target to change dynamically with each message, similar to using semicolons in message boxes.
  • multiplex~ The multiplex~ object functions as a signal multiplexer, allowing you to select one signal from multiple inputs to be passed to a single output.
  • pan~ pan~ performs circular or azimuth panning of an input signal across a user-defined number of output channels.
  • demux The demux object acts as a demultiplexer, routing data from its left inlet to one of its multiple outlets.
  • pan4~ The pan4~ object is a 4-channel equal power (sine/cosine) panner designed for quadraphonic audio.
  • delace~ The delace~ object deinterleaves a multichannel audio signal, splitting it into multiple separate signal outlets.
  • xgate.mc~ The xgate.mc~ object routes a multichannel audio input to a selected multichannel output.
  • relay The relay object routes incoming messages based on their first element.
  • matrix~ The matrix~ object routes and mixes audio signals from multiple inlets to multiple outlets.
  • unpack The unpack object splits an incoming message or list into its individual atoms, sending each atom to a separate outlet.
  • decode The decode object routes a 1 to a specific outlet and 0 to all others, based on a numerical input.
  • spread.mc~ The spread.mc~ object distributes multichannel audio input across a specified number of output channels using equal power panning.
  • match~ The match~ object compares an incoming signal to a list of numerical arguments.
  • pan2~ pan2~ performs equal-power (sin/cos) panning, distributing a mono audio signal across two output channels (left and right).
  • spray The spray object distributes incoming list elements to its outlets.
  • demux~ The demux~ object acts as a signal demultiplexer, routing a single audio signal from its inlet to one of its multiple outlets.
  • xfade~ The xfade~ object crossfades between two audio sources, each supporting 1 to 64 channels.
  • routetype The routetype object routes incoming messages to different outlets based on their data type.
  • retrieve The retrieve object in Pure Data fetches data from named receive objects (r, else/receiver) or GUI elements that have built-in receive names.
  • route2 The route2 object functions similarly to route, but it preserves the list selector in its output messages and supports both float and symbol arguments for routing.
  • out4~ out4~ is a quadraphonic audio output abstraction for Pure Data, providing click-free control over DSP on/off, mute, and gain.
  • nmess The nmess object acts as a message gate, allowing a specified number of messages (n) to pass through its left outlet.
  • multiplex The multiplex object routes data from one of its multiple inlets to a single outlet.
  • switch The switch object in Pure Data acts as a message router, allowing only one of its 'n' inlets to pass data through to its single outlet at a time.
  • xgate2.mc~ The xgate2.mc~ object routes a single input signal to multiple output channels within a multichannel signal.
  • routeall The routeall object routes incoming messages to different outlets based on matching arguments, similar to route but with enhanced capabilities.
  • spread The spread object routes incoming lists to different outlets based on a comparison of the list's first element to a set of arguments.
  • mux The mux object functions as a multiplexer, directing the signal from one of its multiple input inlets to its single outlet.
  • spread~ The spread~ object distributes multiple input audio channels across multiple output channels using equal power panning.
  • mtx~ The mtx~ object acts as an audio signal matrix router, enabling flexible connections between multiple audio inlets and outlets.
  • xselect2.mc~ xselect2.mc~ is a multichannel signal selector that crossfades between adjacent input channels.
  • next The next object in Pure Data detects whether an incoming message is part of the same "logical event" as the previous one.
  • hot The hot object in Pure Data acts as a message router and temporary storage.
  • receiver The receiver object in Pure Data functions similarly to the vanilla receive object, allowing it to receive messages sent to a specified name.
  • merge~ The merge~ object combines multiple audio signals, potentially with varying channel lengths, into a single multichannel output.
  • dispatch The dispatch object takes a list as input and sends each element to a specified address.
  • osc.route The osc.route object routes incoming Open Sound Control (OSC) messages based on their address patterns.
  • pick~ pick~ selects a specific channel from a multichannel audio connection.
  • selector~ The selector~ object acts as a signal-rate multiplexer, routing one of its 'n' signal inputs to a single output.
  • cycle The cycle object distributes incoming messages to its outlets in a round-robin fashion.
  • pan.mc~ pan.mc~ is a multichannel audio panner designed for circular speaker arrays.
  • split The split object separates incoming float numbers into two outlets based on whether they fall within a specified minimum and maximum range.
  • selector The selector object routes incoming data from one of its multiple inlets to its single output.
  • message The message object in Pure Data serves as a versatile container for storing and outputting any type of message, functioning similarly to a message box but as an object.
  • select~ The select~ object selects one of its input audio signals based on a control input, without applying any crossfade.
  • mtx.mc~ The mtx.mc~ object is a multichannel signal routing matrix for Pure Data, designed to route audio signals from multiple input channels to multiple output channels.
  • suspedal The suspedal object emulates a MIDI sustain pedal, holding Note Off messages when active and releasing them when deactivated.
  • mux~ The mux~ object acts as a signal multiplexer, routing one of its multiple signal inlets to a single signal outlet.
  • mix2~ The mix2~ object is a 2-channel audio mixer that provides independent gain and panning control for each input signal.
  • send2~ The send2~ object transmits audio signals to a corresponding r~ object, similar to send~.
  • bucket The bucket object passes incoming numbers sequentially through its multiple outlets in a rotational pattern, like a bucket brigade.
  • rotate.mc~ The rotate.mc~ object performs equal power rotation for multichannel audio signals.
  • mono The else/mono object manages incoming MIDI note messages to enforce monophonic behavior, allowing only one note to sound at a time based on configurable priority modes (last, high, or low note).
  • voices The voices object manages voice allocation for polyphonic synthesizers, offering advanced features beyond poly.
  • route~ The route~ object, part of the zexy library, separates incoming audio signals from control messages.
  • demultiplex The demultiplex object routes data from its left inlet to one of its outlets.
  • xselect.mc~ The xselect.mc~ object selects a specific channel from a multichannel audio connection, supporting up to 512 channels.
  • xselect2~ The xselect2~ object selects and crossfades between multiple audio input signals.
  • onebang The onebang object acts as a conditional gate for bang messages.
  • mix4~ The mix4~ object is a 4-channel audio mixer abstraction that provides independent gain and panning control for each channel.
  • multireceive The multireceive object allows a single outlet to receive messages from multiple send objects.
  • demultiplex~ The demultiplex~ (or demux~) object takes a single audio or control signal and routes it to one of its multiple outlets.
  • router The router object routes incoming messages from its left inlet to a specific outlet.
  • out.mc~ The out.mc~ object routes multichannel audio signals to specified dac~ output channels, with a default starting channel of 1.
  • chance The chance object outputs a bang to one of its outlets based on a weighted probability distribution.
  • slice~ The slice~ object splits a multichannel signal into two outputs based on a specified split point.
  • xselect~ The xselect~ object selects one of its multiple signal inputs and outputs it.
  • sender The sender object functions similarly to Pure Data's send object, but with enhanced capabilities.
  • get~ get~ extracts one or more specific channels from a multichannel audio connection.

docs.title: bondo
docs.text:
The bondo object in Pure Data synchronizes and routes incoming messages. It can distribute elements of multi-element messages across its outlets or collect messages over a specified time interval before outputting them all at once. This allows for flexible control over message flow and timing within a patch.

docs.title: bucket
docs.text:
The bucket object passes incoming numbers sequentially through its multiple outlets in a rotational pattern, like a bucket brigade. It can be configured with a specific number of outlets and an output flag to control immediate or delayed output. Its shifting direction and output can be controlled via messages such as l2r, r2l, freeze, and thaw.

docs.title: cycle
docs.text:
The cycle object distributes incoming messages to its outlets in a round-robin fashion. Each subsequent message is sent to the next available outlet, wrapping around to the first after the last has been used. It can operate in a default mode, cycling continuously, or an "event sensitive" mode where each new event restarts the output from the first outlet.

docs.title: gemreceive
docs.text:
gemreceive is an ordered message receiver for the GEM (Graphics Environment for Multimedia) environment. It functions similarly to receive but includes an additional priority argument, allowing messages to be processed in a specific order. A higher priority number means the object will receive its message later, which is useful for managing drawing order in graphics.

docs.title: grab
docs.text:
The grab object in Pure Data sends a message to another object and captures its output, routing it through grab's own outlets. It can be configured with a numerical argument to specify the number of outlets, allowing it to grab multiple outputs from a single source. Additionally, grab can be linked to a receive object via a symbol argument, enabling it to retrieve values from objects connected to that receive or even directly from GUI elements, making it useful for data retrieval and preset management.

docs.title: hot
docs.text:
The hot object in Pure Data acts as a message router and temporary storage. When any of its inlets receives a message, it outputs the current values from all its inlets, from right to left. docs.title: matrix~
docs.text:
The matrix~ object routes and mixes audio signals from multiple inlets to multiple outlets. It supports a "binary" mode for simple on/off connections, which can cause clicks, and a "non-binary" mode that allows for gain control and ramp times for smoother transitions. When multiple inlets route to the same outlet, their signals are summed.

docs.title: next
docs.text:
The next object in Pure Data detects whether an incoming message is part of the same "logical event" as the previous one. It outputs a bang from its left outlet if the message is a new event (e.g., a single mouse click or a delayed message), and a bang from its right outlet if it's part of the same event (e.g., multiple bangs from an uzi object). This is useful for triggering actions once per distinct message dump or event stream.

docs.text:

It functions like [send] and [receive] for inter-patch messaging, but extends this capability across separate Pd applications without requiring explicit network or OSC configuration. docs.title: switch
docs.text:
The switch object in Pure Data acts as a message router, allowing only one of its 'n' inlets to pass data through to its single outlet at a time. The leftmost inlet controls which of the other inlets is "switched on" by receiving a float value (0 closes all, positive integers select the corresponding inlet). It can handle any type of message.

docs.title: unmerge~
docs.text:
unmerge~ separates the channels of a multichannel signal into groups of a specified size. Each group is sent to a dedicated outlet, with any remaining channels routed to an additional rightmost outlet. If the input signal has fewer channels than the configured outlets, the unused outlets output zeroes.

docs.title: Samplers
docs.text:
• tabplayer~ tabplayer~ is a multichannel array player for Pure Data, designed for flexible audio playback from tables.
  • grain.sampler~ The grain.sampler~ object is a sample-based granulator that generates clouds of audio grains from a loaded sample.
  • brane.m~ The brane.m~ object is a comprehensive granulator sampler with stretch/compress capabilities and a harmonizer pitch shifter.
  • sfont.m~ The sfont.m~ object is a convenient wrapper around sfont~ designed for soundfont-based synthesis.
  • gran.player~ gran.player~ is a granular audio player that allows for independent time stretching and pitch shifting of loaded sound files.
  • sfont~ sfont~ is a sampler synthesizer that plays SoundFont (.sf2/.sf3) files, based on FluidLite.
  • susloop~ The susloop~ object generates a control signal for reading audio buffers, specifically designed for sustained looping of a section.
  • play.file~ play.file~ is a Pure Data object that reads and plays audio files from your computer, supporting various formats like AAC, FLAC, MP3, and WAV.
  • sample~ The sample~ object creates and manages an audio buffer, enabling users to load, record, save, and manipulate audio samples.
  • play~ The play~ object plays audio from an array, allowing playback of any segment, at varying speeds (including backwards), and with interpolation.
  • residency_buffer~ residency_buffer~ is an audio object that manages a spectral sample buffer, similar to residency~.
  • pix_film The pix_film object loads and plays digital video files, providing frames as textures for use within Pure Data's GEM environment.
  • sfz~ The sfz~ object is a Pure Data synthesizer that plays SFZ instruments, which are text-based definitions for sample-based and hybrid synthesis.
  • player~ player~ is a versatile Pure Data object for playing back audio files.

docs.title: play.file~
docs.text:
play.file~ is a Pure Data object that reads and plays audio files from your computer, supporting various formats like AAC, FLAC, MP3, and WAV. It can also stream audio directly from web links. Similar to Vanilla's readsf~, it handles multichannel audio and offers control over playback, including looping and autostart.

docs.title: player~
docs.text:
player~ is a versatile Pure Data object for playing back audio files. It supports various file formats, multi-channel audio (up to 64 channels), and advanced playback controls such as speed adjustment (including backwards playback), looping, fading, and crossfading. It automatically adjusts for sample rate differences between the audio file and Pure Data's current sample rate.

It supports multichannel files and provides messages for operations like opening, saving, clearing, trimming, and normalizing audio data. Its internal buffer can be accessed by other Pure Data objects for further processing.

docs.title: susloop~
docs.text:

It responds to gate inputs (float or signal) to trigger, stop, pause, or continue playback, and allows setting loop points and playback speed. This object is typically used in conjunction with sample playback objects like tabread4~.

docs.title: Sequencers
docs.text:
• store The store object is an abstraction built upon Pure Data's text object, designed for sequential storage and management of messages.
  • rec The rec object records incoming messages across multiple tracks and plays them back.
  • drum.seq The drum.seq object provides a graphical drum sequencer for creating and playing drum patterns.
  • score The score object is a Pure Data score sequencer that uses a custom text-based syntax to define musical events.
  • euclid The euclid object implements the Euclidean rhythm algorithm, which distributes a given number of "hits" as evenly as possible over a specified number of "steps".
  • seq8.m~ The seq8.m~ object is a simple 8-step sequencer for Pure Data, designed to generate musical sequences.
  • phaseseq~ phaseseq~ is a signal-rate object that generates impulses when an incoming phase signal crosses a predefined list of threshold values.
  • seq The seq object in Pure Data is a MIDI sequencer that can play, record, load, and save MIDI streams to and from MIDI or text files.
  • list.seq The list.seq object sequences through elements of a given list, outputting one element at a time upon receiving a bang.
  • counter The counter object in Pure Data increments or decrements an integer value within a specified range.
  • impseq~ The impseq~ object generates a sequence of impulses.
  • mtr The mtr object in Pure Data functions as a multi-track message recorder and playback system.
  • polymetro~ polymetro~ is a polyrhythmic metronome for signal rate that outputs impulses.
  • sequencer The sequencer object in Pure Data plays back a user-defined sequence of elements, advancing one step per bang.
  • pattern The pattern object is a rhythmic pattern sequencer that outputs note durations based on a defined sequence.
  • score2 The score2 object is a sequencer that plays back musical scores using a rhythmic notation syntax similar to pattern.
  • sequencer~ The sequencer~ object outputs values from a predefined sequence when triggered by a signal (0 to non-0 transition) or a bang.

docs.title: drum.seq
docs.text:
The drum.seq object provides a graphical drum sequencer for creating and playing drum patterns. Users can activate cells in a grid to represent drum hits, and the object outputs bangs to trigger sounds based on the sequence. It supports managing multiple tracks and slots, and allows importing and exporting patterns.

docs.title: impseq~
docs.text:
The impseq~ object generates a sequence of impulses. When triggered by a signal or a bang, it outputs the next value from its internal sequence of floats. The sequence can be set using a list, and specific positions can be jumped to using the goto message, making it useful for rhythmic patterns and triggers.

docs.title: pattern
docs.text:
The pattern object is a rhythmic pattern sequencer that outputs note durations based on a defined sequence. Durations can be specified as floats or fractions (where 1 is a whole note), with negative values indicating rests, and a | symbol separating bars. It supports setting tempo, starting/stopping, and dynamically updating sequences, making it suitable for complex rhythmic generation.

docs.text:
phaseseq~ is a signal-rate object that generates impulses when an incoming phase signal crosses a predefined list of threshold values. It functions as a phase-based sequencer, outputting an impulse and the index of the triggered threshold each time a value is reached. This allows for precise rhythmic or event triggering within a cycle.

  • lfnoise~ The lfnoise~ object generates band-limited low-frequency noise, producing random values between -1 and 1 at a specified frequency.
  • white~ The white~ object generates white noise using a pseudo-random number generator.
  • grain.live~ grain.live~ is a live input granulator that generates clouds of audio grains from an incoming signal.
  • lfnoise lfnoise is a control-rate low-frequency noise generator that outputs random values between 0 and 127.
  • ikeda~ The ikeda~ object is a chaotic signal generator based on the Ikeda map, producing two audio outputs.
  • drunkard The drunkard object generates a sequence of pseudo-random numbers based on a drunkard's walk algorithm.
  • standard~ standard~ is a chaotic signal generator based on the standard map equations.
  • gray~ The gray~ object generates a unique type of noise based on "gray code" (reflected binary code), which results from flipping random bits using a pseudo-random number generator.
  • dust~ dust~ generates random positive impulses at a specified density, based on SuperCollider's "Dust" UGEN.
  • urn The urn object is a unique random number generator.
  • oscnoise~ oscnoise~ is an oscillator that generates a white noise waveform, which can be updated by a bang message.
  • noisi~ The noisi~ object generates bandlimited noise by drawing random numbers at a specified rate in Hz and interpolating between them.
  • lincong~ The lincong~ object is a chaotic signal generator based on a linear congruential difference equation.
  • drunk The drunk object generates a "drunk walk" sequence of random numbers.
  • rand.f~ The rand.f~ object generates random floating-point values within a specified range.
  • decide The decide object randomly outputs either 0 or 1.
  • rand.hist The rand.hist object generates weighted random numbers based on a user-defined histogram, which dictates the probability of each index being output.
  • brown~ brown~ is a brown noise generator, also known as Brownian or red noise, which produces a signal whose spectral energy drops 6dB per octave.
  • scramble The scramble object reorders the elements of an input message.
  • drunkard~ The drunkard~ object generates a random walk signal, stepping within a defined range from its previous output.
  • rand.f The rand.f object generates pseudo-random floating-point numbers within a user-defined range when triggered by a bang.
  • brown The brown object generates control signals based on a bounded Brownian motion, which is a pseudo-random walk.
  • prob The prob object generates a weighted series of random numbers, functioning as a 1st-order Markov chain.
  • rampnoise~ The rampnoise~ object generates band-limited noise by interpolating between random values, producing "random ramps" within a specified frequency range.
  • gendyn~ The gendyn~ object generates audio using Dynamic Stochastic Synthesis, based on Xenakis' 'GenDyn' algorithm.
  • randpulse~ randpulse~ is a random pulse train oscillator that generates an audio signal alternating between a random value and zero.
  • rampnoise The rampnoise object generates control-rate ramp noise, producing a pseudo-random waveform within the 0-127 range, making it suitable for MIDI control.
  • randpulse The randpulse object is a random pulse oscillator that alternates between a random value and zero at a control rate.
  • stepnoise The stepnoise object generates control-rate stepped random values, typically within the 0-127 range, without requiring the DSP to be on.
  • stepnoise~ The stepnoise~ object generates band-limited noise with random, stepped values between -1 and 1.
  • rand.u The rand.u object generates a sequence of unrepeated random integer values within a specified range (0 to size-1).
  • rand.list The rand.list object selects a random element from a given list of floats.
  • pix_noise The pix_noise object generates a uniform noise image, with adjustable width and height.
  • tempo~ The tempo~ object functions as a metronome, generating impulses at a specified tempo in BPM, milliseconds, or Hertz.
  • dust2~ The dust2~ object generates random impulses (values between -1 and 1) at random times, controlled by a density parameter.
  • randpulse2 randpulse2 is a random pulse train oscillator that generates pulses (0 or 1, or random values between -1 and 1) at random intervals.
  • noish~ The noish~ object generates bandlimited noise by drawing a random number every n samples and interpolating between these values.
  • perlin~ The perlin~ object generates 1-dimensional Perlin noise, producing a smoothly varying signal based on a specified frequency in Hertz.
  • pvgrain~ pvgrain~ is a spectral granulator that tracks an input sound and synthesizes grains based on its spectral characteristics.
  • rand.dist The rand.dist object generates random numbers based on a user-defined probability density function (PDF) provided by a Pure Data array.
  • rand.i~ The rand.i~ object generates random integer values within a specified range, triggered by signal transitions.
  • disarrain~ The disarrain~ object functions as a spectrum scrambler, reordering a specified number of frequency bins within an audio signal.
  • crackle~ The crackle~ object generates noise using a chaotic difference equation.
  • velvet~ velvet~ is a velvet noise generator that produces audio by randomly choosing positive (1) or negative (-1) impulses at random positions within a specified frequency period.
  • pink~ The pink~ object generates pink noise, an audio signal characterized by constant power per octave, with its energy decreasing by 3dB per octave.
  • rand~ rand~ generates random values between -1 and 1 at a given frequency, linearly interpolating between these values to produce a bandlimited noise signal.
  • chance The chance object outputs a bang to one of its outlets based on a weighted probability distribution.
  • rand.i The rand.i object generates pseudo-random integer values within a specified numerical range.
  • randpulse2~ randpulse2~ is a random pulse train oscillator that generates signals at random intervals, with the average frequency controlled by a "density" parameter.

docs.title: brown
docs.text:
The brown object generates control signals based on a bounded Brownian motion, which is a pseudo-random walk. It produces a float output within a specified range (default 0-127), with a configurable maximum step size. The sequence can be made repeatable by setting a seed value.

docs.title: chance
docs.text:
The chance object outputs a bang to one of its outlets based on a weighted probability distribution. It can be configured with a single percentage for a binary choice or a list of values to define a multi-outlet histogram. This allows for probabilistic routing of events, and its behavior can be made reproducible using a seed.

docs.title: chance~
docs.text:
The chance~ object probabilistically routes an incoming bang to one of its outlets based on a set of weighted chances. These chances can be defined by creation arguments (acting as a histogram) or dynamically updated via a float or list message. It uses a pseudo-random number generator, which can be seeded for reproducible sequences.

docs.title: drunk
docs.text:
The drunk object generates a "drunk walk" sequence of random numbers. It produces numbers within a specified range (0 to maximum) where each new number differs from the previous one by a random value less than a given step size. You can control the maximum value, step size, and seed the random number generator.

docs.title: drunkard
docs.text:
The drunkard object generates a sequence of pseudo-random numbers based on a drunkard's walk algorithm. It takes a step (positive or negative) from its last output, wrapping the value if it hits defined upper or lower bounds. Users can control the step range, bounds, probability of a positive step, and set a seed for reproducible sequences.

docs.title: gendyn~
docs.text:
The gendyn~ object generates audio using Dynamic Stochastic Synthesis, based on Xenakis' 'GenDyn' algorithm. It creates a waveform whose points undergo a random walk in both amplitude and frequency, allowing control over the number of points, frequency range, amplitude/frequency step percentages, and interpolation type. Users can also specify different probability distributions for the random steps and seed the random number generator for reproducible results.

docs.text:

docs.title: prob
docs.text:
The prob object generates a weighted series of random numbers, functioning as a 1st-order Markov chain. It defines transitions between states using lists of three numbers: value1, value2, and weight. A bang triggers the output of the next state based on the defined probabilities, and its probability matrix can be embedded within the patch for persistence.

docs.title: urn
docs.text:
The urn object is a unique random number generator. It generates integer numbers within a specified range (0 to n-1) without repetition, drawing from a pool of available numbers. It can be seeded for reproducible sequences and provides an outlet to signal when all numbers have been drawn.

AI Generated

  • Download via Deken.

    Open Pd and go to ToolsFind Externals. Search for zexy and install it. Then create an object with declare -lib zexy -path zexy. Finally, use urn or any other object from zexy.

docs.title: velvet~
docs.text:
velvet~ is a velvet noise generator that produces audio by randomly choosing positive (1) or negative (-1) impulses at random positions within a specified frequency period. It allows for control over the polarity bias, time regularity, and amplitude irregularity of these impulses, and supports multichannel operation.

docs.title: Subtractive
docs.text:
• bl.vsaw~ bl.vsaw~ is a bandlimited variable sawtooth waveform oscillator that can also generate triangular waveforms.
  • bl.square~ bl.square~ is a bandlimited square wave oscillator designed for synthesis, providing anti-aliased output.
  • vcf.m~ vcf.m~ is a single-channel lowpass filter module.
  • saw2~ The saw2~ object is a versatile sawtooth wave oscillator that supports negative frequencies and multichannel operation.
  • bl.osc~ bl.osc~ is a bandlimited oscillator that generates various waveforms like saw, triangle, square, and impulse.

docs.title: Text
docs.text:
• symbol2list The symbol2list object converts an incoming symbol into a list.
  • regex-help The regex object checks if an incoming symbol matches a specified regular expression.
  • list2symbol The list2symbol object converts an incoming list into a single symbol.
  • s2l The s2l object converts an input symbol into a list.
  • makesymbol The makesymbol object converts an incoming list (up to 10 members) into a single symbol.
  • fromsymbol The fromsymbol object converts an incoming symbol message into its corresponding Pure Data message type, such as a float, bang, list, or anything message.
  • stack The stack object stores and manages sequences of messages, outputting them one by one upon receiving a bang.
  • msgfile The msgfile object reads and writes messages to text files, extending the functionality of textfile.
  • tosymbol The tosymbol object converts any incoming message (e.g., floats, lists) into a single symbol message.
  • regex The regex object checks if an input symbol matches a given regular expression.
  • freadln The freadln object reads text files line by line, outputting each line as a list.
  • format The format object creates a single symbol (string) by combining a format string with multiple input values.
  • text3d The text3d object in Pure Data renders a single line of text within a 3D graphical environment, leveraging GEM's transformations.
  • unite The unite object concatenates incoming messages and converts them into a single symbol message.
  • comment The cyclone/comment object is a GUI element for displaying customizable text within a Pure Data patch.
  • strcmp The strcmp object compares two lists as if they were strings, similar to the strcmp function in C programming.
  • spell The spell object converts incoming messages, character by character, into their corresponding UTF-8 (Unicode) integer values.
  • messbox The messbox object is an editable GUI element in Pure Data that functions similarly to a standard message box, allowing users to type and output various message types during run mode.
  • atoi The atoi object converts an incoming symbol (string) into a numerical representation.
  • midi2note The midi2note object converts MIDI pitch values into musical note names, such as 'Eb3', including an octave number where middle C (MIDI pitch 60) is C4.
  • dollsym The dollsym object in Pure Data expands dollar-prefixed symbols like $0-x or $1-y into their corresponding values within a patch.
  • lace The lace object interleaves elements from two or more input lists into a single output list.
  • atof The atof object converts an ASCII symbol (text) into a floating-point number.
  • display The display object shows text messages within a Pure Data patch, similar to print.
  • textextruded The textextruded object renders a single line of extruded text within the GEM environment, applying current color and 3D transformations.
  • separate The separate object in Pure Data splits an input symbol (string) into a list of individual elements.
  • uniqsym The uniqsym object removes duplicate symbols from a list or stream, maintaining a unique set of values.
  • fwriteln The fwriteln object writes text files line by line, serving as a simplified version of textfile.
  • note2midi The note2midi object converts musical note names (e.g., 'C4', 'E#+5', 'Db-4') into their corresponding MIDI pitch values.
  • sprintf The sprintf object in Pure Data formats messages similar to C's printf function.
  • note The note object is a GUI element in Pure Data designed to display text notes within a patch.

docs.title: atoi
docs.text:
The atoi object converts an incoming symbol (string) into a numerical representation. It supports decimal, hexadecimal (prefixed with 0x), and octal (prefixed with 0) conversions. If the symbol cannot be converted into a number, it is outputted as a symbol.

docs.title: format
docs.text:
The format object creates a single symbol (string) by combining a format string with multiple input values. It uses C-style printf specifiers (e.g., %d, %f, %s) to define how inputs are converted and inserted, allowing for flexible text generation, such as creating dynamic filenames or messages.

docs.title: fromsymbol
docs.text:
The fromsymbol object converts an incoming symbol message into its corresponding Pure Data message type, such as a float, bang, list, or anything message. It effectively removes the "symbol" selector and parses the symbol's content. The object also supports an @separator attribute to define a custom character for splitting the symbol's content into a list.

docs.title: makesymbol
docs.text:
The makesymbol object converts an incoming list (up to 10 members) into a single symbol. It functions similarly to makefilename but for general symbol creation, allowing for formatting using %s tags in its right inlet. The output is always a symbol, useful for dynamic symbol generation.

docs.title: regex-help
docs.text:
The regex object checks if an incoming symbol matches a specified regular expression. It can be configured for case sensitivity and outputs a float indicating a match, along with a list that likely contains matched groups or substrings.

docs.title: regex
docs.text:
The regex object checks if an input symbol matches a given regular expression. It outputs a float indicating a match and a list containing details about the match, such as its ID, start, and end positions within the symbol. This object is useful for pattern matching and parsing text data.

docs.title: separate
docs.text:
The separate object in Pure Data splits an input symbol (string) into a list of individual elements. It uses a specified separator character, which defaults to a space, but can be configured via a "symbol" message. An empty symbol can be used as a separator to split the input into a list of single characters.

docs.title: spell
docs.text:
The spell object converts incoming messages, character by character, into their corresponding UTF-8 (Unicode) integer values. It can process symbols and lists of numbers/characters, outputting a list of integers. Optionally, the output can be padded to a minimum size with a specified fill character, making it useful for formatting text data.

docs.title: sprintf
docs.text:
The sprintf object in Pure Data formats messages similar to C's printf function. It takes a format string with placeholders (e.g., %d for integers, %s for strings) and creates inlets for each placeholder. Incoming data to these inlets is then formatted and output as a single message, optionally as a symbol using the symout argument.

docs.title: unite
docs.text:
The unite object concatenates incoming messages and converts them into a single symbol message. By default, it uses a space as a separator between elements, but a custom separator can be specified via an argument or the second inlet. This allows for flexible formatting of combined data into a single string.

docs.title: VST2/VST3
docs.text: • vstplugin~ vstplugin~ is part of vstplugin v0.6.0, a project that enables the use of VST2 and VST3 plugins in Pure Data (Pd) on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

docs.title: vstplugin~
docs.text:
vstplugin~ is part of vstplugin v0.6.0, a project that enables the use of VST2 and VST3 plugins in Pure Data (Pd) on Windows, macOS, and Linux. It supports audio, MIDI, and instrument plugins; parameter automation (sample accurate for VST3); multi-bus input/output; native or generic GUIs; preset management; MIDI I/O; tempo and transport sync; offline rendering; and plugin discovery in standard or custom paths. Additional features include bit-bridging, sandboxing, Windows plugin support on Linux via Wine, and optional multithreaded processing.

  • Download via Deken.
  • Library developed mainly by Christof Ressi.
  • Report Bugs/Errors here!
  • Available for .

docs.title: Objects from Help Patches
docs.text:
You are both a technical writer and a classifier.

Task:
    1) Produce a concise, clear English description (2–3 sentences max) of the Pure Data object described by the following help patch. Focus on what the object does, its purpose, and key usage hints if they are evident. Keep it simple and practical.
    2) Score ALL categories (listed below) with a confidence between 0.0 and 1.0 for how well the object's functionality fits each category.

Very important output rules:
- Return ONLY a valid JSON object (no markdown and no extra text).
- The JSON must have exactly these top-level keys:
  - "description": string
  - "scores": object mapping each category name to a float (0.0–1.0)
- The "scores" object MUST include EVERY category key exactly as provided (same spelling).
- Values in "scores" MUST be numbers (floats). If a category does not apply, use 0.0.
- Do not add or remove keys. Do not include comments.
- No trailing commas.

Categories (use as keys in "scores", exactly):
{CATEGORIES_JSON}

Object title:
{TITLE}

Help Patch (.pd text):
`
{PATCH_TEXT}
`

docs.title: pd4web
docs.text:
pd4web enables PureData functionality directly in web browsers. Not just the vanilla Pd, but Pd with all the externals!

Contributors Key Features
  • Streamlined Development: Create fully online audio applications with a visual approach.
  • Easy Access: Performers can access compositions without complex PureData setups or library dependencies.
  • Live Electronic Music Preservation: Explore the potential of WebAudioApps to maintain the integrity of live electronic works. pd4web will download and make a repository for all code you need to run your music.
  • Download here.
  • Developed by Charles K. Neimog.
  • Report Bugs/Errors here!
  • Available for .
Music

docs.title: PdXR

docs.title: purr-data
docs.text:
Purr Data is the latest (2.x) branch of Ivica Ico Bukvic's Pd-l2ork. Pd-l2ork in turn was a fork of Hans-Christoph Steiner's Pd-extended, which has been the longest-running (and arguably the most popular) variant of Miller Puckette's Pd. Pd a.k.a. Pure Data, the common basis of all these variants, is Miller Puckette's interactive and graphical computer music and multimedia environment. Pd is also the premier open-source alternative to Cycling74's well-known commercial Max program (whose original version was also developed by Miller Puckette when he was at IRCAM in the 1980s). There are a few other popular and very capable applications in the realm of computer music and media art, most notably Csound and SuperCollider. But Max and Pd's special appeal is that you work in an intuitive graphical "patching" environment which allows you to put together advanced real-time signal processing applications without having to learn a "real" programming language.

You can use the web version here
